name: Education
tagline: Student information systems, timetabling and education administration software
desc:
Education software is one of CSI's oldest and deepest verticals — Constellation and its operating groups (Volaris, Harris, Jonas, Topicus) together own 17 education software businesses spanning K-12 school administration, university timetabling, education data analytics, school payments, library systems and private school management across New Zealand, Australia, the UK, France, the Netherlands, Germany, the US and Canada.

Volaris leads the education vertical internationally, having built a portfolio through seven+ acquisitions across Australasia alone (MUSAC, TASS, Kinetic/Celcat, Edumate, Prima, Furlong, WebCheckout). Harris governs the US K-12 market through Harris School Solutions — a dedicated sub-brand covering analytics (eScholar), payments (EZSchoolPay), teacher evaluation (Quintessential) and student management (Onhand Schools). Topicus brings European depth through French (Alcuin), Dutch (Topicus Education, Eduarte, DEDACT) and Portuguese (Edubox) education platforms. Jonas contributes through Plurilogic (Quebec private schools).

The education vertical is a textbook CSI play: small national/regional TAMs that deter large horizontal software vendors, government-mandated data standards that lock in incumbents, and multi-year renewal cycles driven by deep institutional integration.
why:
Education software sits at the intersection of complex public-sector regulation, mission-critical student data management, and highly fragmented regional markets — a combination that Microsoft, Salesforce and Oracle find impossible to serve economically. CSI's education portfolio spans K-12 and higher education, covering six continents, with each VBU deeply embedded in its own jurisdiction-specific compliance framework (FERPA in the US, NCEA in New Zealand, APVMA in Australia, CNIL/GDPR in Europe). Switching costs are generational: a school district or university that replaces its SIS disrupts enrolment, attendance, compliance reporting, fee processing and parent communications simultaneously. The result is customer retention curves that resemble those of regulated infrastructure rather than software — exactly the profile CSI prices with its First Chicago four-scenario DCF.
◆ Key Strategy: Why CSI Targets the Education Vertical

The global K-12 and higher education software market is estimated at $13B–$15B, fragmented across hundreds of national and sub-national niche products. No single vendor — not even PowerSchool, Ellucian or Oracle — can serve the complete spectrum of jurisdictions, compliance frameworks, and school types.

CSI's approach: acquire the dominant niche incumbents per region — MUSAC in New Zealand, Celcat in UK higher education, Plurilogic in Quebec private schools — rather than building a single global platform that cannot satisfy any jurisdiction's requirements. Each VBU is too small to attract a serious PE rollup but large enough to generate 25–30%+ EBITDA margins serving customers who cannot credibly switch.

The First Chicago lens on education: the walking wounded scenario (some churn to cloud-native competitors) carries low probability weight because the regulatory switching cost is disproportionately high relative to the value of any feature advantage a competitor might offer. A New Zealand Ministry of Education-accredited SMS vendor like MUSAC is not threatened by Salesforce Education Cloud — it is threatened only by a direct competitor that has already invested years in MoE accreditation, NCEA data structures and local support.
🏛 K-12 Regulatory & Compliance Framework (US)
FERPA — Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act
The US federal law governing the privacy of student education records. FERPA gives parents (and students aged 18+) the right to access, amend and control disclosure of education records. Schools may not disclose personally identifiable information from student records without written consent except to specified parties (school officials, other schools on transfer, federal authorities). Every US K-12 SIS, data warehouse and school payment platform must be FERPA-compliant by design — governing how data is stored, accessed and shared. Violations carry federal funding penalties. eScholar's CDW, EZSchoolPay and Onhand Schools are all FERPA-compliant platforms.
IDEA — Individuals with Disabilities Education Act
Federal legislation requiring public schools to provide a Free Appropriate Public Education (FAPE) to children with disabilities. IDEA mandates Individualized Education Programs (IEPs) for every qualifying student — written documents specifying goals, services, accommodations and placement. Schools are legally obligated to maintain, track, and report IEP data, creating a permanent, compliance-auditable paper trail. SIS vendors that manage special education (SPED) must support IEP creation, progress monitoring, annual review scheduling and regulatory reporting to the state education agency.
ESSA — Every Student Succeeds Act (2015)
The primary federal education law for K-12 schools, replacing No Child Left Behind (NCLB). ESSA devolves accountability to states — each state must develop its own plan for measuring school quality, setting academic goals, and supporting low-performing schools. ESSA-driven accountability requires states to report on multiple indicators: academic achievement, graduation rates, English proficiency (ELL), school quality/student success. State Longitudinal Data Systems (SLDS) and district data warehouses like eScholar CDW are core infrastructure for meeting ESSA reporting obligations.
IEP — Individualized Education Program
A legally binding document developed for every student receiving special education services under IDEA. An IEP specifies the student's present levels of performance, annual measurable goals, special education services, related services (speech therapy, OT, PT), accommodations and modifications, and participation in general education. IEPs must be reviewed annually, with a triennial re-evaluation. Districts must track IEP compliance across hundreds or thousands of students — a data-management burden that drives demand for integrated special education modules within the SIS.
Title I Funding
The largest federal education funding programme, providing billions annually to schools and districts serving high proportions of students from low-income families. Title I funds are allocated by formula (based on census poverty data) and can be used for supplemental instruction, staffing, and technology. Districts receiving Title I funds must report detailed programme effectiveness data to the state — another driver of the data warehouse market. eScholar's CDW integrates Title I programme data with student outcome data to support Title I compliance reporting and programme evaluation.
FRPL — Free and Reduced Price Lunch
A federal eligibility programme under the National School Lunch Act. Students from families at or below 130% of the federal poverty level qualify for free meals; 130–185% qualify for reduced-price meals. FRPL status is a standard demographic variable in education data systems — used both for compliance reporting and as a socioeconomic proxy variable in school quality and student outcome analysis. eScholar's data warehouse and EZSchoolPay's nutrition payment processing both interact with FRPL eligibility data. FRPL identification rates also determine Title I fund allocation.
CIPA — Children's Internet Protection Act
Federal law requiring K-12 schools and libraries that receive E-Rate discounts or certain federal grants to implement Internet safety policies and filtering technology protecting minors from obscene or harmful content. Schools must block or filter internet access to visual depictions that are obscene, child pornography, or (for minors) harmful. CIPA compliance is a prerequisite for E-Rate funding — the federal programme subsidising broadband and technology access for schools and libraries. School IT management platforms like AixConcept must support CIPA-compliant content filtering configurations.
E-Rate (Schools and Libraries Programme)
A US federal programme administered by the Universal Service Administrative Company (USAC) providing schools and libraries with discounts of 20–90% on eligible broadband, telecommunications and internet services. E-Rate funded approximately $3.9B in technology costs for schools in 2024. Eligibility and compliance require detailed technology planning and documentation — often managed within or alongside district SIS and IT management platforms.
SLDS — State Longitudinal Data System
A state-level education data infrastructure linking student records across early childhood, K-12, higher education, and the workforce — enabling policy-makers to track educational pathways and outcomes over time. P20W (Pre-K through Grade 20 to Workforce) SLDS systems are funded by the US Department of Education. eScholar is a recognised P20W SLDS provider, offering the CDW and Uniq-ID products specifically designed to match and link student identities across multiple source systems and over multiple years.
📋 Student Data & Interoperability Standards
SIS — Student Information System
The master operational database for a school or district, storing all core student data: enrolment, demographics, attendance, grades, schedules, behavioural records and special education status. Every other education technology system either integrates with or depends on data from the SIS. Switching the SIS is the most disruptive possible technology change a district can make — affecting every staff member, parent communication, state reporting and financial system simultaneously. Harris School Solutions' Onhand Schools and MUSAC Classic are both SIS platforms.
SMS — Student Management System (Australasia)
The Australasian equivalent of a US SIS — the term used in New Zealand and Australia for the primary school administration platform. Key difference: NZ SMS platforms must integrate directly with the Ministry of Education's national data collections (enrolment data, NCEA results, attendance returns). MUSAC is NZ's leading SMS provider, accredited by the Ministry of Education. The Alpha School System is another Volaris/CSI SMS in the Australian market.
Ed-Fi Data Standard
An open-source data standard for K-12 education technology interoperability, developed by the Ed-Fi Alliance and widely adopted across US states and districts. Ed-Fi defines a canonical data model for student, assessment, staff, and course data — allowing different technology systems to share data in a consistent format. Ed-Fi's Operational Data Store (ODS) and API framework are the primary integration points for modern district data ecosystems. eScholar's CDW and Advantage platform are built on Ed-Fi standards.
SIF — Schools Interoperability Framework
An XML-based data exchange standard for K-12 education software, enabling different systems (SIS, LMS, assessment, nutrition, library) to share data in real time without manual export/import. SIF-compliant software can automatically push and pull data from any other SIF-certified system. eScholar adheres to SIF standards, enabling automated data integration with the district's existing SIS, assessment, and nutrition systems without manual data pipeline management.
CDW — Curriculum Data Warehouse / Education Data Warehouse
A purpose-built data repository for education, consolidating data from multiple operational systems (SIS, assessment, HR, finance) into a longitudinal analytical model. Unlike operational databases optimised for transactions, a CDW is optimised for analytical queries: trend analysis, cohort tracking, programme evaluation and regulatory reporting. eScholar CDW (eScholar's data warehouse product) has served Fort Wayne Community Schools for 20+ years and is used across multiple US states for P20W SLDS implementations.
EWS — Early Warning System
A predictive analytics application that identifies students at risk of academic failure, chronic absenteeism, or dropping out — typically using attendance, grades, behaviour and assessment data to generate risk scores. EWS allows counsellors and administrators to intervene proactively rather than reactively. eScholar's myTrack product is an EWS built on the CDW data model. Fort Wayne Community Schools' Go Green graduation-readiness tracker is a real-world EWS implementation powered by eScholar.
NCEA — National Certificate of Educational Achievement (NZ)
New Zealand's main qualification for senior secondary school students (Years 11–13). NCEA is standards-based: students accumulate credits by achieving against individual standards in each subject, rather than sitting end-of-year exams. NZ schools must track each student's credit accumulation, literacy and numeracy requirements, and University Entrance (UE) eligibility in real time. MUSAC Classic and MUSAC edge are deeply integrated with NCEA data structures — tracking credit accumulation, identifying cusp students (those close to achieving a qualification level), and generating MoE compliance returns.
🏫 Higher Education Jargon
Timetabling / Academic Scheduling
The process of assigning courses, instructors, rooms and time slots across an academic term — subject to hundreds of constraints: room capacities, instructor availability, student module registrations, laboratory requirements, equipment needs, and institutional policy rules (no back-to-back lectures in different buildings, no classes before 9am, etc.). Large multi-faculty universities may have tens of thousands of timetabling events per term. Manual timetabling at this scale takes weeks; Celcat AI reduces this to hours. Timetabling software is deeply embedded in university operations — changes require reintegration with the student record system, room booking systems, and HR pay claim systems.
SRS — Student Record System
The higher education equivalent of a K-12 SIS — the primary system of record for student enrolment, module registration, grades, awards and academic progression. Common UK SRS platforms include SITS:Vision (Tribal) and Banner (Ellucian). Celcat Timetabler integrates with all mainstream SRS and MIS platforms, receiving module registration data to drive timetable demand and publishing final timetables back to the SRS for student visibility.
Pay Claim / Hourly Pay
In UK further and higher education, many institutions employ casual or fractional teaching staff (hourly-paid lecturers, visiting tutors) who are paid based on timetabled contact hours and marked registers. Generating accurate pay claims requires matching timetabled events against attendance registers and submitting aggregated hours to payroll. Celcat's Pay Claim module automates the entire process — from timetabled event to verified attendance to payroll submission — replacing error-prone manual spreadsheet workflows.
Room Utilisation / Space Utilisation
A key performance metric for university estates departments measuring how efficiently teaching spaces are being used: what percentage of timetabled slots are actually occupied, and at what average seat occupancy. Poor room utilisation (common in UK HE — often 40–60%) represents significant wasted capital. Timetabling software like Celcat generates room utilisation reports that inform estate strategy, identify underused buildings, and justify capital investment or divestment decisions.
HESA — Higher Education Statistics Agency (UK)
The UK body that collects and publishes data on the higher education sector. UK universities must submit annual HESA data returns covering students, staff, qualifications, estates, and finances. HESA data underpins university league tables, government funding allocation, and research assessment. Timetabling and SRS systems must produce HESA-compliant data extracts — a regulatory lock-in that makes switching costs high for UK higher education institutions.
♞ CSI Strategic Fit — Why Education is a Textbook Constellation Play Education software combines every element CSI screens for: fragmented national TAMs, deep regulatory lock-in, government-mandated data standards that increase switching friction over time, and mission-critical workflows where system failure affects students, parents, staff and regulators simultaneously.

1. Jurisdiction-Specific Compliance Creates Permanent Moats
MUSAC is accredited by the NZ Ministry of Education — a status that took years to achieve and that a new entrant cannot replicate quickly. Celcat's deep integrations with UK MIS platforms (SITS, Tribal) and HESA reporting requirements are not features that can be bolt-on replicated; they represent decades of domain-specific engineering. eScholar's Ed-Fi and SIF certifications, P20W SLDS implementations, and 25-year customer relationships at state level are regulatory infrastructure, not software features. Each of these represents a durable competitive moat in its jurisdiction.

2. Small TAMs Per Jurisdiction — Too Small for Big Competitors
The NZ school SMS market has approximately 2,500 state schools. The UK higher education timetabling market covers ~170 universities. The Quebec private school management market covers ~330 private establishments. Each of these is individually too small to justify a Microsoft or Oracle product team — but large enough to sustain a profitable, mission-critical VMS business with 25–30%+ EBITDA margins. CSI buys these incumbents rather than competing with them, because no global player can justify the investment to dislodge them.

3. How the First Chicago Lens Applies to Education VMS
Winner: Continued market leadership in the jurisdiction, modest feature expansion (AI-assisted timetabling in Celcat, early warning analytics in eScholar), and price increases tracking CPI — generating compounding FCF over 20+ years.
Modest Winner: Stable installed base, flat-to-low growth, strong margins. School and university budgets are funded by government — churn driven by budget cuts rather than competitive displacement.
Walking Wounded: A cloud-native competitor gains traction in the jurisdiction over 5–10 years. Even then, regulatory re-accreditation requirements and integration complexity slow any migration.
Wipe-Out: Regulatory change eliminates the compliance burden that creates lock-in. Historically rare in education — if anything, data standards have multiplied over time (Ed-Fi, SIF, SLDS, HESA), increasing integration complexity and switching costs for incumbents.

4. Sub-Group Compounding Chain
The Volaris Education portfolio (MUSAC, Celcat/Kinetic, TASS, The Alpha School System, Edumate, Plurilogic via Jonas) collectively covers NZ primary/secondary, Australian secondary/tertiary, UK higher education, French private schools, Dutch education, and Portuguese education — each too small to acquire individually as a PE target, but collectively representing a durable, globally diversified education software platform that CSI holds forever.
buyers: School district CIOs, superintendents, university IT heads, ministry of education directors

Automated CVE patching reduces the window of vulnerability exposure.

github.name: GitHub Dependabot — Celcat AI Dependency Management
github.desc: Celcat AI's cloud-native platform uses Dependabot to keep its AI scheduling engine, graph database client libraries and Angular/React frontend dependencies automatically updated — critical for a UK higher education SaaS platform where any downtime during timetabling season (September enrollment) is unacceptable.

ml.name: AI Early Warning System — Predicting Student Dropout & Chronic Absenteeism
ml.desc: ML models on eScholar CDW longitudinal data generate student risk scores integrating 15+ indicators: attendance patterns, assessment trajectory, grade trends, discipline records, ELL status and FRPL eligibility. Districts using these models report earlier identification of at-risk students — months before a counsellor would otherwise flag them — enabling targeted intervention funding and tracking under ESSA accountability frameworks.

ml.name: AI Academic Timetabling Optimisation — Celcat AI Graph Engine
ml.desc: Celcat AI's scheduling engine uses advanced AI algorithms and a graph database to solve timetabling as a constraint-satisfaction optimisation problem. The system considers thousands of variables simultaneously: instructor availability, room capacities, module co-enrolment patterns, student travel times between buildings, laboratory equipment access and institutional policy rules. Output: clash-free timetables in hours, with real-time what-if scenario modelling for mid-term changes.

ml.name: AI NL Query — Conversational Access to Student Data (eScholar Advantage)
ml.desc: Natural language query layer on eScholar Advantage lets district administrators and principals ask plain-English questions of the data warehouse: 'Which Year 10 students are below grade level in maths and had more than 10 absences last term?' — without writing SQL or navigating complex filter trees. Results are returned with FERPA audit trails. Democratises data access for non-technical school leaders.

ml.name: AI Payment Fraud Detection — EZSchoolPay School Nutrition Integrity
ml.desc: ML anomaly detection on EZSchoolPay payment transaction streams flags suspicious patterns: accounts adding unusually large amounts, duplicate payment submissions, abnormal low-balance-to-payment cycles suggesting potential FRPL misclassification or account fraud. Helps district nutrition directors maintain programme integrity under USDA school nutrition compliance requirements.

ml.name: NCEA Learning Analytics — MUSAC edge Achievement Trajectory Modelling
ml.desc: MUSAC edge ML models analyse NCEA credit accumulation trajectories for NZ secondary school students, identifying cusp students (those on the threshold of achieving Level 1/2/3 or University Entrance) weeks before the end-of-year assessment period. Counsellors receive targeted student lists for intervention, improving qualification completion rates and MoE compliance reporting.

ml.name: AI Timetable Demand Forecasting — Celcat + SRS Integration
ml.desc: Predictive models analyse historical module enrolment patterns, room booking data and student registration trends to forecast timetable demand for the upcoming academic year before student registration opens. Enables Celcat users to pre-allocate room capacity, identify potential clash hotspots and plan staffing requirements — reducing reactive timetable re-work during the high-pressure September enrollment window.

name: Software development
tagline: Development frameworks, low-code platforms and developer productivity tools
desc:
The Software Development vertical — framed internally by Volaris as Developer Platforms — is CSI's most technically differentiated vertical: rather than building software for a specific industry, these companies build the tools that developers and businesses use to build software.

The four anchoring businesses define the portfolio: 4D SAS (France, 2024) — a 40-year hybrid low-code RAD platform with 4D and Qodly; PC Soft (France, 2025) — WinDev/WebDev/WinDev Mobile cross-platform dev tools used by Honda France, Ferrero and Casio; EASA (UK/US, 2022) — Excel-to-web-app automation platform; and Reprise Software (US, 2022, Vela) — RLM software licensing management for ISVs. WennSoft provides field service ERP on Microsoft Dynamics for skilled trades.

Volaris has now acquired two French developer tools companies (4D SAS, PC Soft) within 7 months in 2024–25, signalling a deliberate Developer Platforms acquisition thesis. The common thread: mature, mission-critical dev tools embedded in production applications that customers have built their businesses on for 20–40 years — creating perhaps the deepest switching cost profile in all of enterprise software.
why:
Developer tools and development platforms occupy a unique position in CSI's VMS thesis: the switching cost is not merely operational disruption — it is the complete rewrite of every application a customer has ever built on the platform. A company that has spent 10 years building its core business application on 4D's 4th Dimension cannot switch to Microsoft Power Apps without rewriting 10 years of 4D language, schemas, and ORDA data models from scratch. Every ISV that has embedded RLM into their product must refactor their entire licensing architecture to switch. Every engineer who has wrapped their MATLAB/Excel model in EASA must rebuild that application in a different low-code platform, revalidate every calculation, and re-certify every workflow. These are not software migration costs — they are software product reconstruction costs.
◆ Key Strategy: The Developer Platforms Thesis — Why CSI Is Building a French Dev Tools Portfolio

The global application development platform market is estimated at $30–45B, dominated by Microsoft (Power Platform, Visual Studio), Salesforce (Force.com), Google (AppSheet), OutSystems and Mendix. But within this vast market there are dozens of niche incumbent platforms — development environments that carved out specific communities of practice 20–40 years ago and have been the foundation of thousands of production business applications ever since.

4D SAS (founded 1984) and PC Soft (founded 1984) are precisely this profile: two French companies that built cross-platform development tools in the 1980s, accumulated thousands of ISV and corporate developer customers who built real business applications on their platforms, and then operated independently for 40 years — continuously improving their platforms while their customers accumulated 10–30 years of application investment that cannot be economically rewritten. Volaris acquired both within 7 months (Nov 2024 and May 2025), under the same Portfolio Manager (John Isak, Developer Platforms), signalling a deliberate vertical rollup thesis.

The First Chicago lens: the wipe-out scenario for a 40-year development platform requires not just a competitive product, but the obsolescence of 40 years of customer application investment simultaneously — an event that has not historically occurred in developer tools (COBOL customers still exist; 4GL customers still exist; FoxPro customers migrated over 15 years, not overnight). The winner scenario sees sustained renewal revenue from the installed base, with incremental growth from Qodly (4D's cloud-native play) and WebDev (PC Soft's cloud-native play) attracting new developer cohorts.
💻 Developer Tools Terminology
RAD — Rapid Application Development
A software development methodology emphasising fast prototype development, iterative design and user involvement over extensive upfront planning. RAD environments like 4D's classic platform combine an integrated database, IDE, language runtime and deployment environment into a single tool — dramatically reducing the time from concept to working application compared to traditional programming. 4D was designed from the start as a RAD environment for business applications: it combines 4D Language (a high-level proprietary language), an integrated SQL-compatible database, ORDA (Object Relational Data Access) and a built-in web/API server in one product. ISVs who build products on 4D can ship business applications that would take months to build in Java or .NET in days or weeks.
Low-Code / Hybrid Development Platforms
Software development tools that reduce manual coding through visual tools, pre-built components and declarative logic — enabling faster application development by both professional developers and "citizen developers." The spectrum: no-code (pure drag-and-drop, no programming knowledge required) → low-code (primarily visual with coding optional) → hybrid low-code (full programming language + visual tools). 4D and Qodly occupy the hybrid end — they provide both a visual development environment and a full programming language (4D Language), enabling complex business logic that pure no-code tools cannot handle. PC Soft's WinDev is similarly hybrid. EASA is effectively a no-code tool for Excel users: the "code" is the Excel spreadsheet itself.
Cross-Platform Development — Write Once, Deploy Anywhere
The capability to write application source code once and deploy it across multiple target platforms (Windows, macOS, Linux, web, iOS, Android) without significant code duplication. PC Soft's WinDev/WebDev/WinDev Mobile suite is explicitly designed for cross-platform deployment: a developer writes application logic in PC Soft's WLanguage and can deploy to desktop (WinDev), web (WebDev) or mobile (WinDev Mobile) with the same codebase. This dramatically reduces development cost for businesses needing to support multiple platforms — and creates deep platform dependency: switching means rewriting every application from scratch in a different language.
ISV — Independent Software Vendor
A company that develops and sells software products for use by end customers, as distinct from custom development shops or internal IT teams. ISVs are a primary target customer for both 4D (which powers ISV applications across healthcare, legal, real estate and manufacturing) and Reprise Software (whose RLM product enables ISVs to license and protect their own software products). The ISV relationship is particularly sticky for development platforms: once an ISV has shipped a commercial product built on 4D or WinDev, migrating requires rebuilding and re-certifying the entire product — a multi-year engineering project that the ISV will only undertake if forced by the platform's discontinuation. CSI's "hold forever" philosophy directly addresses this risk.
Software Licensing Models — The ISV Revenue Architecture
The mechanism by which ISVs control and monetise access to their software products. Core licensing models: Node-locked (licence tied to a specific machine's hardware ID — most restrictive, traditional); Floating/concurrent (a pool of licences shared across users, checked out and returned at runtime — most common for enterprise/engineering software); Named user (specific users assigned fixed licences); Trial/demo (time-limited evaluation); Subscription (recurring payment for ongoing access); Usage-based/token (pay-per-use or consume tokens from a pool). Reprise RLM supports all of these from a single API — the key commercial advantage over rolling proprietary licensing code.
RLM — Reprise License Manager
Reprise Software's flagship software licensing toolkit for ISVs. An ISV integrates RLM into their product via a single C API call; at runtime, the application checks out a licence from the RLM licence server. Key capabilities: floating licence pools, hardware-locked licences, usage tracking and reporting, redundant server failover, virtual machine detection, encrypted digitally-signed licence files. Competing product: FlexNet (Flexera/Revenera) — the dominant alternative that Reprise explicitly positions against as a simpler, more ISV-friendly and lower-cost alternative. Multiple customers cite switching from FlexNet to RLM as a cost and complexity reduction move.
Excel-to-Web Automation — EASA's Core Technology
The process of wrapping a complex Excel spreadsheet (including VBA macros, custom add-ins and complex formulas) in a browser-based web application front-end — so that end users access the spreadsheet's calculation logic through a controlled web interface rather than the raw Excel file. The underlying Excel file runs on a server; users see only the web interface and cannot directly access or modify the spreadsheet logic. Key enterprise benefits: version control (one master spreadsheet on the server, not hundreds of copies), IP protection (competitors and users cannot see the formula logic), audit trail (every calculation is logged), role-based access control, database integration and workflow management. EASA's unique positioning: full Excel compatibility including VBA — unlike most low-code tools that require rewriting the logic.
Microsoft Dynamics GP vs Business Central — WennSoft's Pivot
Microsoft Dynamics GP (formerly Great Plains) is a legacy on-premise ERP platform with a large installed base in North American mid-market businesses. Microsoft announced end-of-life for Dynamics GP feature development (security patches extend to April 2031). Dynamics 365 Business Central (BC) is its cloud-native successor. WennSoft (Volaris) has built on Dynamics GP for 25+ years and must navigate this transition: its Signature suite runs on GP (committed through 2031), while ProjectStream 365 is purpose-built for Business Central. This migration path represents both a retention risk (GP customers must eventually migrate) and a growth opportunity (the Power Platform-native ProjectStream 365 positions WennSoft for the next decade of Microsoft Dynamics deployments in the skilled trades sector).
4GL — Fourth-Generation Language
A high-level programming language closer to human language than procedural languages like C or Java, designed to specify what should be done rather than how to do it. 4D Language (the programming language within 4D SAS's platform) and WLanguage (within PC Soft's WinDev/WebDev/WinDev Mobile) are both 4GL-era languages — they provide high-level constructs for database access, UI generation, form design and business logic that would require thousands of lines of lower-level code. The 4GL era produced many successful business application platforms that still power significant commercial software today (Progress OpenEdge, used by Portfolio+ and other CSI companies, is another). The switching cost from a 4GL is not just rewriting code — it is rebuilding the developer's entire conceptual model of how to build applications.
CPQ — Configure, Price, Quote (EASA Application)
A sales tool that helps companies configure complex products, calculate accurate pricing and generate customer quotes — often replacing complex Excel pricing models maintained by sales teams. EASA explicitly targets CPQ as a primary use case: enterprises with sophisticated Excel-based pricing calculators (VBA, complex formulas, multiple sheets) can wrap them in EASA to create a web-based CPQ tool that salespeople access through a browser without access to the underlying pricing logic. EASA CPQ customers include insurance companies (rating models), industrial manufacturers (engineer-to-order pricing), and financial services firms (structured product pricing). The EASA CPQ pattern is particularly sticky: the pricing model IS the business logic, and migrating it means revalidating every formula and risk factor in a new environment.
♞ CSI Strategic Fit — Software Development Portfolio Analysis 1. 4D SAS + PC Soft: The French Developer Platform Rollup
The acquisition of two French development tools companies within 7 months — both founded 1984, both specialising in cross-platform business application development — is the clearest signal that Volaris has identified a specific sub-niche: legacy development platforms with embedded ISV and corporate developer communities. These are not general-purpose developer tools competing with GitHub Copilot or Microsoft VS Code. They are established platforms that existing production applications depend on — often for 15–30 years. 4D's developer community includes ISVs who have built and shipped commercial healthcare, legal, real estate and logistics applications on 4D Language; replacing those applications requires product reconstruction, not migration. PC Soft's WLanguage powers ERP systems at Honda France, Ferrero and Casio — production systems that generate revenue every day. The "hold forever" thesis is perfectly aligned: there is no exit strategy for the acquiree because there is no realistic competitive threat that destroys 40 years of installed base simultaneously.

2. EASA: IP Protection for the Enterprise Spreadsheet
EASA occupies a structurally unique niche: it is the only platform that web-enables existing Excel VBA/macro-enabled spreadsheets without requiring the logic to be rewritten. This is not a feature — it is a fundamental architectural choice with profound switching cost implications. Every EASA deployment represents an enterprise that has decided its Excel-based pricing model, engineering calculator, insurance rater or financial model is too valuable to rebuild and too risky to expose as a raw spreadsheet. The EASA application IS the enterprise's IP in executable form. Replacing EASA means revalidating every formula, re-certifying every calculation, and rebuilding every workflow — a project that typically exceeds the EASA licence cost by an order of magnitude. P&G's 2004 adoption (still a customer 20+ years later) illustrates the long-tail of EASA's switching-cost moat.

3. Reprise Software (Vela): The ISV Licensing Infrastructure Play
Reprise RLM is to software licensing what MUSAC edge is to New Zealand school SMS: the technical infrastructure that enables a commercial software product to exist as a product. Without a licence manager, an ISV either ships its software unprotected (intellectual property theft risk) or builds its own licensing system (engineering cost, maintenance burden, security risk). RLM solves all three problems from a single API call. The switching cost is embedded in the product architecture of every ISV customer: Tecplot (50,000+ users, 12 years) and Datamine (mining software, 20 countries) cannot remove RLM from their products without rebuilding their entire commercial licensing and activation architecture. FOG Software Group's strategy of deploying RLM across the Vela STEM portfolio ("eating our own dog food") creates an additional compounding advantage: every sister company that adopts RLM is a captive long-term customer.

4. WennSoft: Microsoft Ecosystem Lock-In in Skilled Trades
WennSoft's 25-year position as the leading field service and job cost ERP for Microsoft Dynamics GP users illustrates CSI's value in platform transitions: the business was acquired at the moment when its core platform (GP) is approaching end-of-life, and Volaris's "hold forever" commitment provides the capital stability needed to execute a 5–10 year dual-platform strategy (Signature on GP + ProjectStream 365 on Business Central) without the commercial pressure of a PE owner seeking a 5-year exit. WennSoft customers — HVAC, mechanical, electrical and plumbing contractors — have workflows deeply embedded in Signature's job costing, dispatching and MobileTech field app. Migrating to a generic ERP means retraining every dispatcher, technician and project manager simultaneously. Volaris's stability enables WennSoft to offer customers a credible, long-term GP support commitment (to 2031) while building the Business Central path — exactly the kind of patient capital deployment that creates durable customer relationships.

cos.name: PVX Plus Technologies Ltd.
cos.entity_desc:
Business application development platform — the runtime engine underlying Jonas Club Software. Develops and distributes PxPlus (formerly ProvideX), a feature-rich, platform-agnostic 4GL-derivative language and development suite for building cross-platform business applications: Windows, Linux/Unix, web (iNomads, Webster+) and mobile from a single codebase. Language heritage: PxPlus → ProvideX → Business Basic (a 1970s-era class of languages optimised for business data processing, also including MAI Basic Four, Thoroughbred Basic and BASIS BBx). PVX Plus acquired the ProvideX business from Sage Software in October 2010 and has led its development since. Founder and President Michael King, 25+ years as product visionary.

The Jonas Club Connection: Jonas Club Software (Gary Jonas Computing Ltd., founded 1990) built its entire suite of 60+ club management applications in ProvideX/PxPlus — covering tee-time booking, member accounting, F&B POS, catering, reservations, events and CRM for 2,300+ clubs in 20+ countries. By acquiring PVX Plus (March 2023), Jonas brought the development language that powers its own flagship product line in-house. The acquisition was announced by Jim Fedigan, CEO of the Club and Hospitality Portfolio at Jonas — not a generic technology executive — confirming the strategic club linkage. Jonas now owns both the applications and the language runtime they run on: a CSI-paradigm vertical integration that simultaneously protects Jonas Club's technology stack and ensures that the PxPlus platform's roadmap serves the needs of Jonas's 30+ year installed base of club, construction and hospitality applications.

PxPlus key tools: Nomads (GUI framework for desktop apps), iNomads (HTML5/Ajax/JavaScript web runtime for existing PxPlus apps), Webster+ (modern web UI framework), WindX Client (thin-client remote GUI access), PxPlus IDE (VS Code extension available), ODBC/MySQL/Oracle/ADO/DB2 integration, OAuth2 security, Two-Factor Authentication. PxPlus 2025 v22 (latest release) and PxPlus 2026 preview in active development. Global user community including banking, medical records, casino management, club management and ERP applications. Acquired by Jonas March 31, 2023.

azure.name: Azure OpenAI — AI-Assisted Code Generation within 4D and PC Soft Platforms
azure.desc: GPT-4o integration into 4D's IDE and PC Soft's WinDev/WebDev development environments enables AI-assisted code completion, documentation generation, and test scaffolding in 4D Language and WLanguage — proprietary languages that general-purpose AI models have limited training data for. By fine-tuning on the 4D and WLanguage documentation, API references and community forum data, Azure OpenAI can provide meaningful code suggestions without exposing customer source code to public AI training. Qodly (4D's cloud-native platform) is architected to natively integrate AI-generated component design.

azure.name: Azure OpenAI — EASA's AI-Enhanced Excel Model Analysis
azure.desc: Azure OpenAI can analyse the structure and logic of Excel VBA/macro models uploaded to EASA, auto-generating documentation, identifying hard-coded values that should be database-driven, and suggesting EASA application architecture patterns. For enterprise customers with hundreds of Excel models awaiting EASA migration, AI-powered model analysis reduces the discovery and scoping phase from weeks to hours — directly accelerating EASA's sales cycle and customer onboarding experience.

azure.name: Azure AI — Intelligent Licence Usage Analytics (Reprise RLM)
azure.desc: Azure Machine Learning on Reprise RLM's detailed usage logs reveals patterns ISVs can act on: which features are actually used vs licensed (upsell/downsell signals), which customers are approaching concurrent licence limits (expansion opportunities), and which trial users have usage patterns predictive of conversion (sales prioritisation). RLM Cloud already runs on AWS; Azure AI integration enables ISVs to build their own licence intelligence dashboards — transforming RLM from a compliance tool into a product-led growth engine.

azure.name: Azure + Power Platform — WennSoft ProjectStream 365 Cloud Extension
azure.desc: WennSoft's ProjectStream 365 is built on Microsoft Power Platform with Dynamics 365 Business Central — making it natively extensible with Azure services. Azure IoT Hub integration enables WennSoft's Building Optimization Broker (BOB) to ingest real-time building equipment telemetry for predictive maintenance scheduling. Power BI Embedded delivers job cost, technician utilisation and fleet analytics dashboards to WennSoft customers without leaving the application. Azure Logic Apps automates multi-step workflow triggers between field dispatch, job costing and customer invoicing.

azure.name: Azure Static Web Apps + Azure Functions — EASA Cloud Migration
azure.desc: EASA's next-generation architecture targets Azure-hosted deployment: Excel calculation engines run as Azure Functions (serverless, scalable), EASA web applications are served as Azure Static Web Apps, and calculation results are stored in Azure SQL or Cosmos DB. This Azure-native EASA architecture enables enterprise customers to deploy EASA applications within their existing Azure compliance boundary (SOC 2, ISO 27001, FedRAMP) — addressing the top objection from financial services and pharmaceutical customers with strict data residency requirements.

github.name: GitHub Copilot — Accelerating 4D Language and WLanguage Development
github.desc: 4D Language and WLanguage (PC Soft) are proprietary languages with limited training data in standard GitHub Copilot models. Volaris can work with Anthropic/GitHub to fine-tune Copilot on 4D and WLanguage open-source repositories and documentation — creating a specialised copilot that provides meaningful autocompletion in these languages. This would be a significant competitive differentiator for 4D SAS and PC Soft: proprietary language platforms that offer native AI coding assistance retain developers who might otherwise migrate to mainstream languages with stronger AI tooling.

github.name: GitHub Actions — Automated Security Testing for RLM-Integrated Products
github.desc: ISVs using Reprise RLM to protect their software IP can implement GitHub Actions CI/CD pipelines that automatically test licence enforcement at every code commit — verifying that trial limitations are correctly enforced, that floating licence checkout/return works correctly, and that the licence file cryptographic signatures remain uncompromised. Automated licence validation testing prevents the costly product security incidents that occur when a code change accidentally bypasses licence checks — a scenario that costs ISVs revenue and creates legal liability.

github.name: GitHub Copilot Enterprise — WennSoft ProjectStream 365 Development
github.desc: WennSoft's ProjectStream 365 is built on Microsoft Power Platform (Power Apps, Power Automate, Dataverse) — an environment where GitHub Copilot Enterprise provides strong coding assistance. Copilot accelerates the development of custom ProjectStream 365 connectors, Power BI report templates, and Azure Logic Apps workflows that WennSoft's professional services team builds for customer implementations. Faster implementation delivery reduces WennSoft's PS cost per customer while improving time-to-value — directly addressing WennSoft's primary competitive positioning against generic Dynamics implementations.

github.name: GitHub Advanced Security — Protecting ISV Intellectual Property
github.desc: For EASA and 4D SAS, whose products are themselves development platforms that run ISV and enterprise customer application code, GitHub Advanced Security provides essential secret scanning to prevent API keys, database credentials and proprietary algorithm parameters from being accidentally committed to code repositories. For 4D SAS specifically, where ISV customers build commercial products on 4D Language, protecting the ISV's proprietary application logic from exposure is a contractual obligation — Advanced Security's secret detection capabilities are part of the platform's IP protection story.

ml.name: AI Code Migration — 4D Legacy Application Modernisation
ml.desc: The biggest long-term opportunity for 4D SAS is AI-assisted migration of legacy 4D Language v3–v14 applications to modern 4D v20+ and Qodly architectures. LLM-based code translation models can analyse legacy 4D Language code, identify deprecated patterns and propose equivalent modern API calls — dramatically accelerating customer migrations that would otherwise take years of manual rewriting. This is the 'AMOS X acceleration' strategy applied to developer tools: AI accelerates migration from legacy platform to modern platform within the same vendor ecosystem, reducing churn without requiring a full rewrite.

ml.name: AI Model Validation — EASA Financial and Engineering Calculations
ml.desc: EASA customers in financial services (insurance raters, structured product pricing) and engineering (stress analysis calculators, thermodynamic models) have regulatory validation obligations for their calculation models. AI-powered test generation on Azure creates comprehensive test suites that validate EASA application outputs against expected ranges — flagging edge cases where VBA logic produces unexpected results before the model is deployed to end users. This AI validation layer addresses EASA's most significant enterprise procurement friction: 'how do we trust a web interface to an Excel model we can't see?'

ml.name: AI Licence Optimisation — Reprise Usage Pattern Intelligence
ml.desc: ML models on Reprise RLM usage logs identify the optimal licence configuration for each ISV customer's user population: is a floating pool of 10 licences appropriate, or would 8 node-locked + 2 floating be more cost-effective? Do token-based licences make more sense for seasonal usage spikes? AI-driven licence optimisation becomes a value-added service that Reprise can offer ISVs — helping them price and package their own software more effectively while deepening the RLM relationship from a technical dependency to a strategic partner.

ml.name: AI WLanguage / 4D Language Documentation Generation
ml.desc: PC Soft's WLanguage and 4D SAS's 4D Language both have extensive but imperfectly documented APIs, with many features documented primarily in French. LLM-based documentation generation can automatically produce comprehensive English-language API references, code examples and best practice guides from the existing French documentation and source code — dramatically lowering the barrier for non-French-speaking developers to adopt WinDev and WebDev. Better English documentation is the single most frequently cited barrier to international adoption of French-origin developer tools.

ml.name: AI Predictive Job Costing — WennSoft ProjectStream 365
ml.desc: ML models trained on WennSoft historical job cost data predict final job costs at project initiation — before any work has been done — based on job type, scope parameters, customer history and seasonal factors. For HVAC and mechanical contractors managing dozens of simultaneous projects, predictive job costing surfaces cost overrun risk weeks before it materialises, enabling proactive scope conversations with customers and resource reallocation decisions. WennSoft's 25+ years of Dynamics GP job cost data provides an unusually rich training dataset for this model.

name: Local government

desc: Local government software underpins daily operations of municipalities, counties, townships and regional authorities. why: Municipal systems store decades of citizen records and financial transactions. Replacement requires council approval, audit compliance and extensive parallel running periods.
buyers: City managers, municipal CFOs, IT directors at counties and municipalities

cos.name: Interfile Filing Ltd.
cos.entity_desc:
South Africa's leading electronic bill presentment, payment and e-Government platform provider — Harris's first acquisition in Africa (November 2, 2023). Legal name: Interfile Filing Ltd. (trading as Interfile). Founded July 2003 by Sheldon Quarmby, Johannesburg, South Africa. ~131 employees. BEE (Black Economic Empowerment) structure: 69% of shares held by BEE partners.

Every citizen in South Africa uses at least one Interfile creation — paying tax (SARS eFiling), obtaining an ID card/passport, filing for unemployment (uFiling), or paying local municipal bills. The company's tagline: 'To Make a Difference.'

Flagship projects:
▶ SARS eFiling — Created in 2003 for the South African Revenue Service. SARS eFiling transformed tax compliance in South Africa and made SARS one of the most efficient revenue collection agencies in the world. URL: efiling.co.za
▶ eTshwane — Digital municipal services platform for the City of Tshwane (COT), widely regarded as the Smartest City in South Africa
▶ uFiling — Unemployment Insurance Fund electronic filing platform
▶ City of Johannesburg — municipal services payment platform
▶ InterCash — front-end payment processing for invoice presentment and cash receipting for municipalities and government departments

Core solutions: Opti-Channel EBPP (Electronic Bill Presentment and Payment — web, mobile, WhatsApp, over-the-counter) · e-Government digital transformation (passport applications to parking ticket payments) · Smart City platforms · Digital Modernisation consulting and business process re-engineering · Private Cloud Hosting (secure data storage, disaster recovery, managed application services) · Revenue Enhancement · Claims Processing Services · Indigent Solutions · Fraud Prevention · Payment and Reconciliation Solutions · Branch Office Solutions.

Harris integration: CEO Kennedy Chinganya (appointed May 2024, ICT industry veteran, 20+ years experience), reporting to Portfolio Leader Dennis Asbury (EVP, Harris). Harris team oversight: initially Canada, moved to UK team from July 2024. Interfile can now draw on Harris's global developer pool from the UK and Canada for deployment capacity in South Africa.

SADC expansion: Chinganya's stated growth thesis targets Southern African Development Community countries — Botswana, Namibia, eSwatini, Lesotho and Zimbabwe — applying Interfile's municipal revenue collection and e-government model across the region. Harris acquisition (November 2023) provides the financial stability and global resource pool to underwrite this expansion.

cos.name: GEMMS (Gateway Electronic Medical Management Systems)
cos.entity_desc: Specialty EHR and practice management software built exclusively for cardiology, pain management, orthopedics and gastroenterology practices. GEMMS ONE — the current platform name — provides clinical documentation, scheduling, e-prescribing, revenue cycle management, and MIPS/quality reporting automation. Acquired by Harris Computer (CSI) on 24 March 2017; now operates within the Harris Ambulatory Group alongside Digichart. Founded 1997 by Dr. Rodger Pinto in Niagara Falls, NY; serves cardiology practices across 35+ US states. Formally listed in the 2024 CSI Annual Information Form as 'Gateway Electronic Medical Management Systems Inc.'
cos.aif_page: Harris — 2024 AIF (Gateway Electronic Medical Management Systems Inc.)

cos.name: Harris Data Integrity Solutions
cos.entity_desc: Provider of patient data integrity services and software for healthcare organisations, specialising in the identification and elimination of duplicate medical records (MPI de-duplication) and ongoing data quality management. Formed under Harris Healthcare through the combination of Just Associates and QuadraMed's data integrity division; acquired by Harris Computer on 3 June 2013. Products include the Intelligent MPI Triage Solution for rapid duplicate record cleanup. Serves hospitals, health systems and physician practices requiring HIPAA-compliant patient data accuracy.

cos.name: Medaptus
cos.entity_desc: Charge capture, revenue reconciliation and patient assignment software for hospitals and physician groups. KLAS award winner for mobile charge capture. Products: Charge Pro — charge capture, reconciliation, compliance rules engine and analytics (Java/MSSQL/AWS); Assign — intelligent patient-to-provider assignment and rounding workflow automation with ED/Obs acceptance; Charge Infusion — automated infusion coding and billing (Fresenius Kabi partnership); Hospital Medicine Analytics — provider productivity, charge lag and operational reporting. Customers include Banner Health and Peterson Health; integrates with Epic, athenaOne, Cerner/Oracle and Meditech. Near-term AI signals: Assign One (expanded modular patient assignment), Ambient Listening (voice-to-text clinical documentation), Predictive Analytics (missed revenue and denial risk surfacing), Automated Rule Adjustments (AI proposes rule fixes). Acquired by Volaris Group. Part of Volaris healthcare portfolio alongside Sansio.

name: Logistics
tagline: Supply chain management, distribution and logistics operations software
desc: Logistics software manages the movement of goods from suppliers through warehouses to customers — covering transportation management, warehouse management, order management, route optimisation and carrier integrations. Vela operates Friedman, ProShip, Varsity Logistics and Juniper brands. Topicus's Primeur provides enterprise data logistics and integration software used by major organisations globally. Pantheon Automatisering and Transport & Automotive Network Systems serve European logistics businesses.
why: Logistics systems integrate with carriers, warehouses, ERPs and customer order systems. Years of rate tables and carrier relationships cannot be cleanly migrated without significant operational disruption.
buyers: VP Supply Chain, logistics directors, warehouse operations managers, distribution centre heads

name: Housing finance agencies
tagline: Mortgage technology, loan pricing and secondary market software for lenders
desc: Housing finance software manages the origination, pricing, processing and secondary-market execution of residential mortgages and government-backed housing loans. Perseus Group's Optimal Blue is the dominant real-time mortgage pricing engine in the US, processing millions of loans daily against investor guidelines. Dark Matter Technologies handles mortgage origination. ReverseVision serves reverse mortgage lenders. Topicus's Five Degrees provides digital banking infrastructure to European housing finance institutions.
why: Mortgage pricing engines integrate with dozens of investor systems. Switching during volatile rate environments is operationally impossible. Historical loan files have regulatory requirements spanning 7+ years.
buyers: Mortgage company CTOs, secondary market heads, housing authority IT directors

name: Public housing authorities
tagline: Housing management, tenant services and public housing operations software
desc: Public housing authority software manages the complete lifecycle of subsidised and social housing — from applicant waiting lists through tenant management, rent calculation, maintenance work orders, inspection scheduling, compliance reporting and financial management. Emphasys (Vela) is a leading US public housing software provider serving Housing Choice Voucher and public housing programmes for HUD-regulated authorities. Topicus's Hercules Social Housing serves the Dutch social housing sector.
why: Housing authority systems contain waitlist data, tenant records and HUD compliance documentation going back decades. Any migration risks HUD audit findings and federal funding jeopardy.
buyers: Housing authority executive directors, IT directors, HUD programme managers

name: Hospitality
tagline: Hotel PMS, restaurant management and hospitality operations software
desc: Hospitality software covers the full operations of hotels, restaurants, bars, conference centres and food & beverage operations. Jonas leads with Springer-Miller Systems (luxury hotel PMS), Ariane Systems (self check-in), B4Checkin (online pre-arrival) and Impos (Australian hospitality POS). Volaris brings SSP (travel retail), Hospedia (in-room entertainment and food ordering), Catertrax (institutional catering) and Taranto (foodservice). These systems process every guest transaction across 24/7 operations.
why: PMS systems contain years of guest profiles, rate contracts and group blocks. Moving a hotel from one PMS to another requires 6-12 months of parallel operation and staff retraining.

name: Marine asset management
tagline: Vessel management, port operations and maritime enterprise asset management
desc: Marine asset management software manages the complex maintenance, compliance and operational requirements of shipping fleets, offshore platforms and port facilities. SpecTec's AMOS — part of Volaris — is the industry-leading maritime ERP, managing planned maintenance, procurement, crewing, HSEQ and regulatory compliance for thousands of vessels worldwide. Topicus's QPS provides hydrographic survey and offshore positioning software used by survey companies and port authorities globally.
why: Vessel maintenance records are legally required by flag-state authorities. SpecTec's AMOS has 20+ years of vessel history in most installations. Switching risks compliance gaps that can ground vessels.
buyers: Fleet technical managers, DPA (Designated Person Ashore), port operations directors, offshore OIM

cos.name: SpecTec Ltd.
cos.entity_desc: Maritime fleet and asset management software serving ship owners, operators and technical managers for 40+ years (founded 1985). Flagship platform AMOS™ covers maintenance management, procurement, inventory, quality & safety, crew management, dry dock planning and fleet analytics. Trusted by commercial bulk, container, offshore and mixed-fleet operators globally; 30 offices across 30 countries (consolidated post-Volaris acquisition 2012). Key customers include Grimaldi Group. New unified platform AMOS X (.NET/Angular) in active development, targeting full migration by end of 2026. 19 engineers (Argentina + Italy). Near-term AI opportunities: Virtual Superintendent (AI chatbot over vessel manuals/documentation), AMOS Vision (AI image analysis for condition/defect assessment), Compliance & Risk Intelligence (automated regulatory monitoring for ISM/SOLAS/MARPOL). Listed in CSI 2024 AIF as SpecTec Ltd. + Binary System S.r.l.

cos.entity_desc: Corporate, holding, or financing entity in CSI’s corporate structure, governed in Cyprus. Direct website match available from the provided source files.

cos.name: Helm Operations Software Inc.
cos.entity_desc: Cloud-based maritime fleet management SaaS (Helm CONNECT) covering maintenance, compliance, personnel and logistics/dispatch for harbour towage, port authorities, offshore supply vessels, ferry and inland towing operators. MARPOL-approved e-logbooks certified by Lloyd's Register. Modular suite: Logistics (dispatch, billing, job tracking), Maintenance (PMS, inventory, requisitions), Personnel (crew scheduling, certifications, payroll, work-rest), Compliance (audits, drills, corrective actions) and Orders (customer self-service portal). FADE fatigue-scoring module live with Port Hedland (mandatory crew rest compliance). Near-term: Dispatch Copilot (AI route/load optimisation, $300K–$500K annual value per port). 100% daily AI tool adoption across dev team. Listed in CSI 2024 AIF as Helm Operations Software Inc.

name: Para transit operators
tagline: Demand-responsive transit scheduling, dispatch and paratransit operations software
desc: Paratransit software manages demand-responsive transportation for passengers with disabilities, elderly riders and medical trips — a federally mandated ADA-complementary service in the US. name: Fleet and facility management
tagline: Fleet tracking, maintenance scheduling and facilities management software
desc: Fleet and facility management software tracks, maintains and optimises physical assets — vehicles, equipment and built environments — across their full lifecycle. AssetWorks (Volaris) is a leading North American provider of fleet management software for government agencies and universities, managing hundreds of thousands of vehicles. Baseplan serves equipment rental and heavy construction. Smartrak provides GPS fleet tracking and compliance across Australasia.
why: Fleet systems hold decades of asset histories, depreciation schedules and maintenance records. Integration with finance and compliance workflows creates deep operational dependency.
buyers: Fleet managers, facilities directors, VP Operations, public works departments

cos.name: AssetWorks Fleet
cos.entity_desc: Fleet management ERP covering the full vehicle lifecycle — maintenance, work orders, fuel tracking, warranty/recall management, and capital planning. Two product lines: FA (Fleet Anywhere) — hosted/on-prem mid-market platform with mobile companion AW Connect; and M5 — true SaaS for enterprise fleets (USPS-scale). 500+ software customers including city, county, state and federal organisations. Acquired by Volaris/CSI from MAXIMUS; City of New York (27,000 vehicles) and Texas DOT are landmark customers. Near-term AI roadmap: Smart Work Order Agent (photo/voice-driven work order creation), VIN decoder, warranty & recall monitor, and predictive maintenance. Part of the Volaris AssetWorks portfolio alongside Facilities, Appraisal, Higher Education and Risk Management divisions.

cos.name: Smartrak Australia Pty Ltd.
cos.entity_desc: Fleet management and telematics platform for government, health and emergency services in Australia and New Zealand. Two product lines: Smartrak GPS Telematics (IoT vehicle tracking, driver safety, fleet analytics via wired/OBD devices over cellular) and Poolcar (shared vehicle booking and pool management, acquired ~2018). Typical customers: local councils, health organisations (Plunket), police, fire. Fleets of 100+ vehicles. In development: Sentinel — Enterprise Intelligence Layer with AI-powered dashboards, NL query, anomaly detection, auto-generated reports and what-if fleet modelling. Revenue target: $125K by July 2026 from Sentinel add-on; 5/5 customer validation. GitLab mono-repo, spec-driven agentic dev workflows. Part of Volaris Omegro portfolio. cos.name: AssetWorks Facilities
cos.entity_desc: Full lifecycle facilities asset management software (CMMS/EAM) for higher education and K-12 school districts. New product in development: Keystone (capital planning & project management) — prototype built in 4 days using AI agent workflow; closes the asset lifecycle loop when assets near end-of-life, feeding capital replacement planning. Target MVP end of April/May 2026; projected 7-figure annual revenue. Also exploring Workday integration (near-term) and Ops Copilot (AI assistant for shop supervisors). Spec-driven AI development model adopted since mid-Dec 2025; using Claude Code with Jira MCP connectors. AWS-hosted. Part of Volaris AssetWorks portfolio alongside Fleet, Appraisal, Higher Education and Risk Management divisions.

name: Private clubs and daily fee golf courses
tagline: Club management, tee-time booking and private club operations software
desc:
Private club and golf course software manages the operations of private member clubs, country clubs, daily-fee golf courses and golf resorts. Core modules include tee-time booking, handicap management, member account management, food & beverage POS, event management and pro shop retail. Jonas Club and Club Caddie are market-leading platforms in North America and internationally. These are high-touch member-facing environments where the software holds decades of member history and account receivables.

PxPlus Runtime Note: Jonas Club Software's entire 60+ application suite is written in PxPlus (formerly ProvideX) — the Business Basic-derivative language developed and distributed by PVX Plus Technologies (Jonas, acquired March 2023). By acquiring PVX Plus, Jonas brought the language runtime that powers its own club software platform in-house, ensuring the PxPlus roadmap serves Jonas Club's long-term needs. See the Software Development vertical for the full PVX Plus profile.
msize: $1.2B global golf and private club software (2024)
why: Private club systems hold member accounts, lifetime transaction histories and billing relationships going back decades. Switching creates service disruptions that are politically sensitive in member-governed clubs.
buyers: Club general managers, club board technology committees, golf course operators

cos.name: Club Caddie
cos.entity_desc:
Award-winning, fully cloud-based (Microsoft Azure) golf course management software — Jonas's purpose-built play for the daily fee, public and semi-private golf market that Jonas Club Software (private clubs, legacy PxPlus) does not natively serve. Founded by Jason Pearsall (CEO) and Ian Brigmann, both golf course owners/operators with technology backgrounds, who resolved to build enterprise-grade software that operators can actually use. Based in Detroit, Michigan. Pre-acquisition investor: Solidea Capital. Acquired by Jonas Software February 10, 2020; founders and leadership team retained.

Platform: 100% cloud-hosted on Microsoft Azure — no on-site servers required. Single database, single sign-on across all modules. Monthly system enhancements. PCI compliant, P2P encrypted, API-open with authentication. Also builds software for driving ranges and golf simulator businesses.

Core modules: Golf Shop POS · Restaurant POS · Mobile POS (iOS/Android, works offline) · Tee-Sheet (all tee time types: daily fee, member, annual pass, senior, group, maintenance) · Online Booking Engine · Third-party tee time distribution (PGA Tee Times, GolfBack, Teeoff.com, Supreme Golf, GolfBook, GolfNow — all optional, 100% operator price control) · Member Management (automated billing monthly/quarterly/annual, member portal, ACH/card tokenised in PCI gateway) · Banquet/Wedding/Event Management (contracts, deposits, communications, cost/profit analysis) · Mobile App (GPS rangefinder, on-demand delivery coordination, scorecard, reservations, event registration, coupons) · Managed Marketing Services (MMS — email, SMS, website) · Golf Course Website Builder · Unified Inventory (food, beverage, golf, merchandise, hard goods) · G/L Accounting, Revenue Analytics, Member A/R, Golfer Spend, Rounds Played reporting · Time Clock · Staff Permission Controls (role-based, per-button granularity).

reputation.badge: Founded by Golf Operators · Azure-Native · Award-Winning

quotes.text: I couldn't be more pleased with the addition of Club Caddie at our golf course. We are saving so much money and yet getting a much superior service. The customer service has been phenomenal — everyone is responsive to our questions and needs.

Out of 201 participants in our JR Golf Program, 95% of the children were registered online. I really enjoy how easy it is to just check a golfer in for golf or check in and add food & beverage to their tab.

Joining the Jonas family will provide Club Caddie with the resources and experience to become a world-class software provider. The acquisition will allow us to keep doing what we love, while under guidance from a vertical-based software giant.
quotes.role: Jason Pearsall — Founder & CEO, Club Caddie

stats: Together with Jonas Club Software: 2,500+ golf clubs served across 20 countries
Founded by golf course owners/operators — every feature tested by PGA Professionals, GMs, F&B Directors and Event Coordinators before release
100% Azure-hosted — no on-site servers; monthly system enhancements and live training multiple days/week at no additional cost
Jason Pearsall (CEO) and Ian Brigmann (Co-founder) both retained post-Jonas acquisition — founding team still runs the product
Competes directly with GolfNow (NBC Sports Next), ForeUp and Club Prophet in the daily fee/public course market
