name: Student information systems
tagline: Student records management, attendance tracking and academic reporting software
desc:
Student Information Systems (SIS) — also called Student Management Systems (SMS) in Australia and New Zealand, or Management Information Systems (MIS) in the UK — are the operational backbone of every school and university. They store and govern every piece of student data: enrolment, attendance, grades, transcripts, timetables, behaviour records, special education status and parent communications.

CSI's SIS portfolio spans three operating groups across five countries: Volaris owns TASS (400+ Australian independent schools) and Symplicity (1,200+ global universities, acquired Feb 2026); Harris governs Harris School Solutions in the US (eScholar, Onhand Schools, EZSchoolPay); and Topicus holds the dominant Dutch school SMS platforms Somtoday (secondary) and Parnassys (primary).

The SIS vertical is among the highest-switching-cost markets in all of education software. Migrating a SIS disrupts every school function simultaneously: state reporting, attendance, grades, parent communications, scheduling and financial systems all depend on it. CSI's regional incumbents (TASS in Australian independent schools, Somtoday in Dutch secondary schools) have been embedded in their markets for 20–35 years.
why:
The SIS is the system of record for every student in a school — the single source of truth from which all other technology platforms draw data. An SIS sits at the centre of a school's digital ecosystem: every learning management system (LMS), assessment platform, parent communication tool, financial system and regulatory reporting pipeline connects to or depends on it. This makes the SIS simultaneously the most strategically critical and most difficult-to-replace piece of education technology in any institution. CSI's SIS portfolio spans K-12 (TASS, Onhand Schools, Somtoday, Parnassys) and higher education (Symplicity, eScholar), with each VBU deeply embedded in its own jurisdiction's data standards and compliance requirements.
◆ Key Strategy: Why SIS Is CSI's Highest-Switching-Cost Market

The global K-12 and higher education SIS market is estimated at $3B–$5B annually, dominated by PowerSchool, Infinite Campus, Ellucian and Oracle in large public markets — but highly fragmented in niche segments: Australian independent schools (TASS's domain), Dutch state schools (Topicus), Quebec private schools (Plurilogic) and US state-level data systems (eScholar).

CSI's angle is jurisdiction capture: acquire the market-leading SIS in each niche jurisdiction, then hold it forever. TASS is the platform-of-choice for Australian independent schools; replacing it means re-entering data for 400+ schools' entire student histories, rebuilding all state reporting integrations, retraining every administrator and migrating every parent portal. The total cost of switching exceeds any SaaS pricing advantage a competitor could offer.

The First Chicago four-scenario lens on SIS: the wipe-out scenario requires a competitor to replicate years of jurisdiction-specific compliance work (ACARA, ATAR, NAPLAN in Australia; CITO, DUO in the Netherlands; FERPA, Ed-Fi, state data systems in the US) in a market too small to justify that investment. The walking wounded scenario plays out over a decade, not quarters — exactly the timeframe CSI prices at a discount in its DCF models, creating asymmetric upside when customer retention proves better than the model assumed.
📋 Core SIS Functions & Terminology
Enrollment / Enrolment Management
The process of managing a student's entry into a school — from application and waitlist through to formal enrolment, placement in a year group and home class, and issuance of a student ID. In the US spelling is "enrollment"; Australian, New Zealand and UK systems use "enrolment." An SIS manages the entire enrolment workflow: online applications, document collection, fee invoicing, timetable assignment and initial data population. TASS covers the complete enrolment lifecycle for Australian independent schools; Topicus Somtoday handles enrolment for Dutch secondary schools under DUO (Dienst Uitvoering Onderwijs) regulations.
Gradebook — Electronic Grade Recording
The digital tool through which teachers record and report student grades. A gradebook within an SIS captures summative and formative assessment results, links them to curriculum standards, calculates GPA (US) or aggregated results, generates progress reports and feeds into formal transcripts. In the US context, gradebook data must feed into state assessment reporting; in Australia, NAPLAN and ATAR results integrate with the school's SMS gradebook. TASS combines gradebook with its teaching and learning portal. Topicus Somtoday provides teachers with grade entry and report generation for Dutch secondary schools.
Transcript Management
The official permanent academic record of a student's course history, grades, attendance and qualifications. Transcripts are required for higher education applications, employment verification, credit transfer and regulatory audits. An SIS must maintain transcript data with full data integrity across the student's entire school career — often 13 years of primary through secondary education. Transcript generation must comply with jurisdiction-specific requirements (NCEA transcripts in NZ via MUSAC, ATAR-linked transcripts in Australian states, GCSE/A-level records in the UK, state graduation requirements in the US).
Attendance Tracking & Roll Marking
Real-time recording of student presence, lateness and authorised/unauthorised absences. Modern SIS platforms support multiple attendance methods: teacher roll marking in class, parent portal absence notification, whole-school lockdown reporting, and automated alerts for chronic absenteeism. FERPA (US) and its Australian/NZ equivalents mandate accurate attendance record-keeping. eScholar CDW's early warning system uses longitudinal attendance patterns as a primary predictor variable for at-risk student identification. MUSAC edge enables teachers to mark rolls on mobile devices with real-time sync to the school's MoE attendance data returns.
Master Schedule / Academic Scheduling
The process of building the complete course-teacher-room-period schedule for a school year — allocating every course section to a time slot, assigning teachers and rooms, and resolving all conflicts. For a large secondary school with 1,500 students and 80+ staff, a master schedule involves thousands of constraint-satisfaction decisions. The master schedule is the core integration point between the SIS (which holds student course requests and enrolments) and the timetabling software (which builds the schedule). TASS manages scheduling for Australian independent schools; Celcat integrates with SRS/MIS platforms for university timetabling.
Student / Parent / Staff Portals
Web and mobile interfaces that give each stakeholder group role-appropriate access to SIS data. Parent portals display student grades, attendance, report cards, fee statements and school communications. Student portals show personal timetables, assessment results and teacher messages. Staff portals provide administrative tools without requiring access to the full SIS back-end. TASS provides dedicated portals for all three groups plus TASS Orbit mobile apps. Topicus Somtoday's student and parent apps are downloaded by millions of Dutch families annually. Portal adoption is a key metric for parent engagement and a switching-cost multiplier — once parents are habituated to a portal, any change creates visible community disruption.
State / Federal Compliance Reporting (US)
US states require districts to submit detailed student data to the state education agency (SEA) on regular cycles — covering student demographics, programme participation (Title I, SPED, ELL, FRPL), attendance, assessment results and staff qualifications. The SIS is the data source for all these submissions. Non-compliance can result in loss of federal Title I or IDEA funding. SIS vendors must maintain certified integrations with each state's data submission system. Harris School Solutions' eScholar CDW is a P20W SLDS provider certified for state-level longitudinal data system implementations; its Ed-Fi connectors automate state compliance extracts from any SIS.
Ed-Fi Integration in SIS Context
Ed-Fi is the US data standard that governs how SIS data is exchanged between district systems, state data systems and third-party tools. An Ed-Fi-compliant SIS can automatically push student enrolment, attendance, grade and assessment data to state Ed-Fi Operational Data Stores (ODS), eliminating manual exports. eScholar's connectors are Ed-Fi certified, enabling automated data pipelines from any district SIS to the eScholar CDW and onwards to state SLDS reporting systems without manual intervention. Ed-Fi adoption is growing across US states as a compliance infrastructure standard, creating long-term lock-in for certified SIS and data warehouse vendors.
ACARA / NAPLAN (Australia)
ACARA (Australian Curriculum, Assessment and Reporting Authority) is the federal body that governs the Australian curriculum and national assessment programme. NAPLAN (National Assessment Program — Literacy and Numeracy) is the annual standardised test administered to Years 3, 5, 7 and 9. Australian state school SMS platforms must submit NAPLAN registration data, pre-populate student details for the online assessment platform (NAP) and receive results back into the SMS for reporting. TASS's RESTful API integrations support NAPLAN data flows alongside state-level reporting for each Australian state education department (ACARA, DET, DESE).
DUO / BRP (Netherlands)
DUO (Dienst Uitvoering Onderwijs — Education Executive Agency) is the Dutch government body responsible for managing student enrolment records, exam registrations and educational funding for all Dutch schools. Dutch secondary schools must report student enrolment, attendance and examination results to DUO via BRON (Basis Register Onderwijs). Topicus Somtoday and Parnassys are built around DUO compliance — all student data flows are structured to meet BRON submission requirements. The BRP (Basisregistratie Personen — Municipal Personal Records Database) integration ensures student identities are verified against the national population register at enrolment.
Employability / Career Services (HE)
University career services platforms manage the transition from study to employment — connecting students with internships, graduate roles and alumni networks while helping institutions demonstrate ROI on the degree investment. Metrics tracked include internship placement rate, graduate employment rate, time-to-first-job, and employer satisfaction scores. Symplicity (Volaris, acquired Feb 2026) is the global leader in this space: 1,200+ universities, 7M+ students, 11M+ job applications and 1.1M internship placements annually. Skills-based hiring matching (aligning student competencies to employer requirements) is Symplicity's core AI-driven product evolution.
♞ CSI Strategic Fit — SIS Portfolio Analysis 1. Jurisdiction Dominance Creates Permanent Moats
TASS serves 400+ of Australia's approximately 1,000 non-government (independent and Catholic) schools. Topicus Somtoday is the #2 education app in the Netherlands and serves the majority of Dutch secondary schools. Parnassys serves the majority of Dutch primary schools. Each of these incumbents has spent 20–35 years building jurisdiction-specific integrations (ACARA/NAPLAN/DET for TASS; DUO/BRON/BRP for Topicus) that a new entrant cannot replicate without years of investment in a market too small to justify that investment.

2. The Symplicity Acquisition (Feb 2026) — A Large-Deal Exception
At 1,200+ university customers and significant scale, Symplicity is larger than CSI's typical acquisition profile — reflecting the evolution toward larger deals noted in CSI's annual reports. The higher-education employability market has proven extremely sticky: once a university's career services team, employer relations staff, students and alumni are embedded in a single platform, switching costs are prohibitive. Symplicity's 96% Fortune 100 employer trust score makes it a two-sided marketplace — any competitor must simultaneously win the university AND the employer side.

3. Data Network Effects in eScholar CDW
eScholar's 25-year data archive at Fort Wayne Community Schools, and its statewide SLDS implementations at the Louisiana Department of Education, represent data network effects that are structurally irreplaceable. A state that has linked 20 years of student longitudinal data through eScholar's unique identifier system cannot migrate without losing that historical linkage — the anchor effect compounds annually, making long-tenured public sector data warehouse customers among CSI's most durable assets.

ml.desc: Symplicity's AI platform matches student competency profiles (derived from degree transcripts, micro-credentials, internship outcomes and extracurricular activity records) to employer job requirements using NLP and skills taxonomy models. The system bridges the education-to-workforce gap for 7M+ students across 1,200+ universities — facilitating 11M+ job applications and 1.1M internship placements annually. Machine learning continuously improves match quality based on placement outcomes and employer feedback signals.

ml.name: AI Attendance Pattern Analysis — NAPLAN Cusp Student Identification
ml.desc: ML models on TASS and MUSAC historical data identify students whose attendance patterns — including specific lesson absences, late marks and school-refusal clusters — correlate with subsequent academic underperformance in NAPLAN or ATAR. These models enable year-level coordinators to intervene before attendance issues translate into qualification-level failures, particularly for Year 11-12 students on the cusp of ATAR score thresholds that determine university admission.

name: Field Service
tagline: Field service management, work order scheduling and technician dispatch software
desc:
Field Service software manages the full lifecycle of work performed outside the office — dispatching technicians, tracking asset maintenance, recording regulatory compliance data and managing service contracts across energy utilities, water companies, police forces, HVAC/mechanical contractors and roll-based manufacturers.

CSI's Field Service vertical spans Jonas, Volaris and Harris across 4 continents: AMT-Sybex (UK/Ireland — utility EAM and mobile field management, 50,000+ devices, 35M meter points), Greycon (global — MES/trim optimisation for roll manufacturers, 500 sites/40+ countries), Sunrise Software (UK — ITSM/ESM with Solvyr® AI), DATABASICS (US/Canada — service contracting ERP), Easit (Sweden — ESM/ITSM), and Bluestar (UK — police/emergency services). Jonas owns the majority of field service VBUs; Volaris holds the two ITSM plays.

Field Service is a prototypical CSI vertical: deeply embedded in regulated industries (Ofwat, Ofgem, ISO 55000 asset management), workflow software that is the system of record for statutory inspection and maintenance obligations, and customers who cannot switch without replacing hard-won regulatory integrations.
why:
Field service software sits at the intersection of physical operations and regulatory compliance. Every water company, energy distribution operator, HVAC contractor and police force runs on work orders — the fundamental unit of service delivery. The software managing those work orders is not a productivity tool; it is a compliance infrastructure. A water utility that loses its asset inspection records faces Ofwat enforcement action. A police force that loses its intelligence management system faces Home Office sanctions. A food equipment contractor that fails to record a gas safety check faces legal liability. This creates a fundamentally different risk calculus for the customer: the cost of switching field service software is not the license fee differential, it is the regulatory exposure of any gap in the operational record.
◆ Key Strategy: Why Field Service Is Among CSI's Most Defensible Verticals

The global Field Service Management (FSM) software market was estimated at $5–6B in 2024, growing at ~12% CAGR — driven by IoT sensor adoption, mobile workforce growth and tightening regulatory compliance requirements. Generic players (ServiceMax, ServiceNow, Microsoft Field Service) dominate broad FSM; CSI's portfolio targets the jurisdiction-specific and industry-specific niches that large vendors cannot serve economically.

AMT-Sybex illustrates the pattern precisely: the company manages data flows for 35 million UK electricity and gas meter points and is deeply integrated with Elexon (UK balancing and settlement code), Ofwat's asset management reporting requirements and SCADA/GIS systems that are unique to UK utility infrastructure. No US FSM software company can replicate this overnight. The integration was built over 25 years; the switching cost is not the software licence, it is the re-engineering of a regulated operational backbone.

Greycon is the complementary manufacturing play: 500 sites in 40+ countries, deeply embedded in the production workflows of every major paper, film and nonwoven manufacturer — industries where a scheduling error causes tonnes of waste material. Queen's Award for Enterprise reflects its genuine global market leadership.
🔧 Field Service Core Terminology
Work Order — The Atomic Unit of Field Service
A work order (WO) is the core transaction in any field service system: a documented request for work to be performed on a physical asset, authorising a technician to attend a site, perform a task, record the outcome and confirm completion. Work orders flow through a lifecycle — raised, planned, scheduled, dispatched, in-progress, completed, closed — and each stage generates a compliance record. In regulated industries, work orders serve as statutory maintenance records: a UK water company's maintenance WOs are evidence of Ofwat asset management compliance; a gas contractor's WOs are the Gas Safe record required by UK law. SAMPro (DATABASICS) and Fieldreach (AMT-Sybex) are both work-order-centric platforms.
Dispatch & Scheduling — Assigning the Right Technician to the Right Job
Dispatch is the process of assigning and routing field technicians to work orders based on skill, location, availability and priority. Modern FSM dispatch uses drag-and-drop scheduling boards, route optimisation, and real-time mobile updates. DATABASICS SAMPro includes a drag-and-drop dispatch board for HVAC/mechanical contractors. AMT-Sybex Fieldreach provides mobile-first work assignment to field crews across UK utility networks. Effective dispatch directly reduces mean time to repair (MTTR) and travel cost — the two primary KPIs for field service efficiency.
EAM — Enterprise Asset Management
EAM software manages the complete lifecycle of physical assets — from design and commissioning through operation, maintenance and eventual replacement or disposal. Unlike a CMMS (which focuses only on maintenance scheduling), an EAM covers asset procurement, capital planning, condition monitoring, depreciation, regulatory compliance and retirement. AMT-Sybex is an EAM specialist: its Ellipse platform manages asset lifecycles for UK infrastructure companies, integrating with GIS, SCADA, telemetry systems and procurement platforms. ISO 55000 (the international asset management standard) is the compliance framework that EAM software must support for regulated infrastructure operators.
CMMS — Computerized Maintenance Management System
A CMMS is a purpose-built maintenance software system tracking assets, scheduling planned maintenance (PM), recording work orders and managing spare parts inventory. DATABASICS SAMPro includes CMMS capability as part of its broader field service ERP. CMMS is differentiated from EAM by scope — a CMMS focuses on maintenance operations, while an EAM covers the full asset lifecycle including capital planning and financial management. The distinction matters commercially: CMMS buyers are maintenance managers; EAM buyers are CFOs and asset directors, commanding larger deal sizes and longer sales cycles.
SLA — Service Level Agreement
A contractual commitment specifying response time, resolution time and service availability guarantees for field service delivery. In B2B service contracting, SLAs are legally binding — breach triggers financial penalties. For HVAC and mechanical contractors using DATABASICS SAMPro, SLA compliance tracking automates the monitoring of response and resolution times against contractual commitments, surfacing at-risk work orders before breach occurs. For IT service management (Sunrise, Easit), SLAs govern IT incident resolution times under ITIL frameworks. The SLA enforcement module is a core switching-cost driver: once a customer's SLA data and breach history lives in a platform, migration means losing that historical SLA performance record.
Preventive Maintenance (PM) & Planned Inspections
Scheduled, calendar-based or usage-based maintenance activities performed to prevent asset failure — oil changes, filter replacements, safety inspections, statutory tests. PM schedules in a CMMS/EAM auto-generate work orders at defined intervals, ensuring regulatory inspection deadlines are never missed. For UK utility companies (AMT-Sybex customers), PM is a Ofwat/Ofgem regulatory obligation: failure to complete scheduled asset inspections on time generates compliance risk and potential enforcement action. For HVAC contractors (DATABASICS), PM contracts are the recurring revenue backbone of the business model — a key economic moat reinforced by the software tracking it.
ITIL — IT Infrastructure Library
The global framework for IT Service Management (ITSM) best practices — defining standardised processes for incident management, problem management, change management, asset management and service desk operations. ITIL compliance is the de facto standard for corporate and public sector IT departments. Sunrise ITSM (Volaris) and Easit GO (Jonas) are both ITIL v4-aligned platforms — meaning their workflow templates, terminology and reporting structures follow the ITIL process model. ITIL certification of a service desk platform is a purchasing requirement for many UK public sector and NHS buyers, creating a qualification barrier that locks out non-compliant competitors.
Trim Optimisation — Greycon's Core Science
In roll-based manufacturing (paper, film, nonwovens, metals), raw material arrives in large master rolls that must be cut into customer order sizes — creating unavoidable trim waste. Trim optimisation software (Greycon X-Trim) calculates the mathematically optimal cutting pattern across all pending orders to minimise waste while meeting order specifications. In the paper industry, a 1% improvement in trim waste can save hundreds of thousands of dollars annually at scale. Greycon optimises 200M+ tonnes per annum in the paper industry alone. The switching cost is extreme: Greycon's optimisation models are calibrated to each customer's specific machine dimensions, order mix and production constraints — any competitor must re-parameterise from scratch.
Ofwat / Ofgem — UK Infrastructure Regulators
Ofwat (Water Services Regulation Authority) regulates UK water and sewerage companies; Ofgem (Office of Gas and Electricity Markets) regulates UK gas and electricity networks. Both impose detailed asset management reporting requirements: Asset Management Plans (AMPs), capital investment programmes and operational performance reporting. AMT-Sybex's Affinity Enterprise and Ellipse EAM platforms are specifically built to generate the asset management records and regulatory reports required by Ofwat and Ofgem frameworks — a deep regulatory integration that makes AMT-Sybex effectively irreplaceable for its UK utility customers without a full EAM programme re-engineering.
ISO 55000 — International Asset Management Standard
ISO 55000 (published 2014, updated 2024) is the international standard for asset management, defining requirements for establishing, implementing and improving asset management systems across an organisation. It applies to any organisation managing physical assets with a material impact on business outcomes — infrastructure operators, utility companies, transport networks, manufacturers. Compliance with ISO 55000 requires demonstrated, auditable asset lifecycle management processes — exactly what EAM platforms like AMT-Sybex Ellipse provide. ISO 55000 certification is increasingly a procurement requirement for infrastructure asset management software in regulated industries.
Meter Data Management & Half-Hourly Settlement (UK)
UK electricity markets settle on a half-hourly basis — every 30-minute period requires metered consumption data from every supply point to calculate network imbalances and trading positions. AMT-Sybex's Meterflow manages meter data for 20M+ meter points; Marketflow processes the associated market data flows (2M records/day) for energy suppliers, network operators and trading companies. These systems are integrated with Elexon's BSC (Balancing and Settlement Code) operational infrastructure — the legal framework governing UK electricity market settlement. No competitor can enter this market without first building BSC-certified integration — a regulatory barrier that took AMT-Sybex 25 years to establish.
♞ CSI Strategic Fit — Field Service Portfolio Analysis 1. AMT-Sybex: Regulatory Infrastructure, Not Software
AMT-Sybex is not primarily a field service software company — it is a regulatory compliance infrastructure provider that runs as software. Its 35-million meter point data management capability, BSC-certified Marketflow platform and Ofwat-integrated asset reporting are statutory requirements for UK energy and water companies. The switching cost is not a software migration; it is a regulatory programme that requires Ofgem/Ofwat pre-approval and a parallel-running period measured in years. This creates permanent customer retention that is immune to feature competition.

2. Greycon: Mathematical Moat in Manufacturing
Greycon's trim optimisation algorithms are calibrated per customer — each paper mill, film extruder or metals processor has unique machine dimensions, order mix complexity and production constraint parameters. Greycon's competitive advantage is not its software architecture; it is 40 years of applied operations research knowledge embedded in production optimisation models that represent millions of hours of customer-specific calibration. The Queen's Award for International Trade reflects genuine global market leadership: 500 sites in 40+ countries, optimising 200M+ tonnes per annum in paper alone.

3. Sunrise Software: AI-First ITSM Incumbent with 1,000+ Customer Data Flywheel
Sunrise occupies a structurally privileged position in UK ITSM: 1,000+ customers — many with 10–20+ year relationships — built during an era when ITSM switching was genuinely painful (custom workflow migrations, ITIL re-certification, SLA data loss). That installed base is now the engine for Solvyr®, Sunrise's AI capability suite launched in 2025. The three live features — Auto Triage (38% handling time reduction), Smart Resolutions (~2 hrs/week saved per analyst) and Knowledge Creator (82% article production cost reduction) — are each trained on years of Sunrise-specific ticket and resolution data that no new entrant can replicate from cold start. Every additional year of customer tenure deepens this data advantage, compounding the AI quality gap between Sunrise and any greenfield competitor.

The CSI lens here is specifically about AI timing: Sunrise acquired Volaris's attention at a moment when ITSM incumbents face their first genuine disruption threat from AI-native competitors (Freshservice, Moveworks). Solvyr® is Sunrise's pre-emptive response — not a bolt-on, but a product built from the multi-year institutional knowledge embedded in customer ticket histories. Sunrise's 10–20 year customer relationships generate far richer training data than any startup can access. The First Chicago winner scenario for Sunrise therefore includes an AI dividend: as Solvyr® improves, churn risk from cloud-native ITSM competitors decreases, and per-seat pricing power increases through AI-tier upsells. The walking wounded scenario assumes AI commoditises ITSM faster than Sunrise can ship features — possible, but the data flywheel advantage makes it unlikely within a 10-year holding horizon.

4. DATABASICS: Service Contracting ERP with 50-Year Regulatory Lock-In
DATABASICS serves a customer base — HVAC, electrical, plumbing, mechanical and commercial food equipment contractors — where the ERP is simultaneously the operational backbone and the compliance record. DCAA (Defense Contract Audit Agency) compliance requirements for US federal contractor customers create a statutory audit trail dependency: every timesheet entry, expense claim and work order is a legal record subject to federal audit. A service contracting firm cannot migrate its DCAA-compliant ERP mid-contract without notifying its contracting officer and potentially triggering an audit review. This regulatory switching barrier is distinct from and additive to the standard operational switching cost — it affects approximately 20–30% of DATABASICS's customer base. The planned Azure cloud-native rebuild (microservices, App Services, Cosmos DB) addresses the key retention risk — aging Windows/SQL Server infrastructure — while preserving the regulatory trust relationships built over 50 years.

azure.name: Azure IoT Hub + Digital Twins — Smart Asset Monitoring for AMT-Sybex
azure.desc: Azure IoT Hub ingests real-time telemetry from AMT-Sybex's 35-million-meter-point UK energy infrastructure — voltage, current, flow rates and equipment health signals — into Azure Digital Twins models that mirror the physical asset network. Anomaly Detection ML models trigger predictive maintenance work orders in AMT-Sybex Fieldreach before asset failure occurs, shifting utility maintenance from reactive break-fix to condition-based planning. This directly supports Ofwat and Ofgem asset health reporting requirements.

azure.name: Azure OpenAI — Intelligent Work Order Generation & Dispatch Recommendations
azure.desc: GPT-4o analyzes incoming service requests (photos, voice descriptions, sensor alerts) and auto-generates structured work orders in DATABASICS SAMPro with pre-filled equipment type, fault code, required parts and skill classification — eliminating manual data entry for dispatchers. The same model recommends the nearest available technician with the right skill certifications, further reducing MTTR. AI-drafted work orders require only a human approval click — the same workflow DATABASICS identified as its Smart Work Order Agent opportunity.

azure.name: Azure Machine Learning — Predictive Maintenance & First-Time Fix Rate Optimisation
azure.desc: ML models trained on AMT-Sybex/DATABASICS historical work order data predict which assets are approaching failure within a 30/60/90-day window, enabling proactive PM work order generation before the asset fails. A second ML model predicts which technician-job combinations have the highest First-Time Fix Rate (FTFR) — matching field crew skills to job requirements to maximise resolution on first visit. FTFR improvement reduces costly repeat dispatch — the single largest cost lever in field service operations.

azure.name: Azure OpenAI — Solvyr® AI Engine Backbone (Sunrise Software)
azure.desc: Sunrise's Solvyr® AI engine is powered by Azure OpenAI, enabling Auto Triage (38% reduction in ticket handling time), Smart Resolutions (~2 hrs/week saved per analyst) and Knowledge Creator (82% reduction in article production cost). Azure's enterprise-grade security and UK data residency on IBM Cloud UK meet NHS and local government data sovereignty requirements. Solvyr® represents the most advanced AI deployment in the CSI Field Service portfolio — a live, measured, customer-validated AI capability with quantified ROI.

azure.name: Power BI Embedded — Regulatory & Operational Dashboards
azure.desc: Power BI Embedded within AMT-Sybex and DATABASICS surfaces real-time operational dashboards to utility asset managers, facilities directors and service operations teams: SLA compliance heatmaps, asset health risk scores, technician utilisation, PM schedule adherence and regulatory reporting metrics for Ofwat/Ofgem submissions. DATABASICS's DBAnalytics module already provides BI dashboards — a Power BI Embedded upgrade enables board-level asset performance visibility without requiring a separate BI tool subscription.

github.name: GitHub Copilot — Accelerating Greycon's Optimisation Algorithm Development
github.desc: Greycon's core competitive advantage is its mathematical optimisation algorithms — written by operations research specialists, not standard software developers. GitHub Copilot Enterprise accelerates the incremental improvement of X-Trim, opt-Studio and D-Opt by generating boilerplate code, unit tests and documentation stubs that free Greycon's specialists to focus on the algorithmic innovation that differentiates their products. Copilot's code completion is particularly effective for the data-processing pipeline code that feeds the optimisation models.

github.name: GitHub Actions — Regulatory Compliance CI/CD for AMT-Sybex
github.desc: AMT-Sybex's Marketflow and Meterflow platforms must maintain certified integration with Elexon's BSC operational infrastructure (UK electricity market settlement) — a regulated integration that changes when Ofgem or Elexon update market codes. GitHub Actions automates regression testing of all BSC integration touchpoints on every code push, flagging breaking changes before they reach production systems that process 2 million market records daily.

github.name: GitHub Copilot — Sunrise Solvyr® AI Feature Development
github.desc: Sunrise Software uses GitHub Copilot to accelerate development of new Solvyr® AI capabilities beyond the three launched in 2025. Copilot assists in generating the ITIL-aligned workflow classification training data pipelines, NLP prompt engineering for ticket categorisation, and the knowledge graph indexing code that powers Smart Resolutions recommendations — helping Sunrise's small development team compete with the AI roadmaps of much larger ITSM vendors (ServiceNow, Freshservice).

github.name: GitHub Dependabot — DATABASICS Azure Rebuild Security
github.desc: DATABASICS's planned Azure cloud-native rebuild (microservices, App Services, Cosmos DB, API Management, Azure AD OAuth 2.0) introduces a modern dependency stack that requires ongoing security maintenance. GitHub Dependabot provides automated vulnerability alerts and PR-based dependency updates across all service packages — critical for a platform processing DCAA-compliant federal contractor timesheet and financial data, where unpatched CVEs can create SOC 2 audit findings.

ml.name: AI Trim Optimisation (Greycon X-Trim) — Operations Research at Industrial Scale
ml.desc: Greycon X-Trim applies advanced mathematical optimisation (integer programming, bin-packing algorithms, constraint satisfaction) to calculate the optimal cutting pattern for master rolls — minimising trim waste while meeting every customer order specification. The algorithm must simultaneously optimise across hundreds of pending orders, each with different width, weight, length and delivery deadline requirements. X-Trim's superiority over generic approaches is demonstrated by its 500-site global deployment and 200M+ tonnes of paper optimised annually. Modern ML improvements add demand forecasting (Greycon Forecasting) and predictive scheduling (opt-Studio D-Opt product cycle optimisation).

ml.name: AI Work Order Routing — Technician Skill & Location Matching
ml.desc: ML routing models on DATABASICS SAMPro and AMT-Sybex Fieldreach historical work order data learn which technician-job combinations produce the best First-Time Fix Rate, shortest job duration and highest customer satisfaction outcomes. The model considers technician skill certifications (Gas Safe, electrical competency, equipment manufacturer certifications), current location (GPS), travel time, parts availability and SLA deadline — outputting a ranked dispatch recommendation that reduces both MTTR and repeat visits.

ml.name: AI Ticket Classification — Solvyr® Auto Triage (Sunrise)
ml.desc: Sunrise Solvyr® Auto Triage uses NLP ML models trained on years of historical ITSM ticket data to automatically classify incoming service requests by category, priority, team assignment and resolution path — achieving a 38% reduction in ticket handling time. The model learns from analyst reassignments and resolution outcomes, continuously improving classification accuracy. For NHS and local government service desks handling thousands of incidents per week, Auto Triage eliminates the bottleneck of manual first-line triage — the most time-consuming and least skilled task in ITSM operations.

ml.name: AI Predictive Maintenance — Condition-Based Asset Management
ml.desc: AMT-Sybex's integration with IoT sensors across UK utility infrastructure (water pump stations, electricity substations, gas pressure regulators) feeds time-series ML models that predict equipment failure 30–90 days in advance based on vibration, temperature, current draw and flow anomaly patterns. Condition-based maintenance (CBM) triggered by these models replaces calendar-based PM schedules — reducing unnecessary maintenance visits while catching failures before they become service outages. For Ofwat-regulated water companies, predictive asset management is increasingly required in Asset Management Plans.

ml.name: AI Knowledge Management — Solvyr® Knowledge Creator (Sunrise)
ml.desc: Solvyr® Knowledge Creator uses GPT-based summarisation to auto-generate knowledge base articles from resolved ticket histories — achieving an 82% reduction in knowledge article production cost. When an analyst resolves a novel issue, the AI drafts a structured KB article (problem description, diagnostic steps, resolution, affected systems) for review and publication. This progressively enriches the Sunrise knowledge base, improving Smart Resolutions accuracy and self-service portal deflection rates — directly reducing inbound ticket volume over time.

ml.name: AI Expense Intelligence — DATABASICS Phase 2 Azure Rebuild
ml.desc: Phase 2 of DATABASICS's Azure cloud-native rebuild targets AI-powered receipt OCR, expense auto-categorisation, policy violation detection and fraud prevention — using Azure Form Recognizer and ML anomaly detection on historical expense pattern data. For US federal government contractors (a key DATABASICS segment), automated DCAA compliance enforcement reduces audit risk: the AI flags expenses that don't meet per-diem or cost-allocation rules before submission rather than discovering violations in post-submission audits.

cos.name: Cornerstone Billing Solutions
cos.entity_desc: Cloud-based billing and account management software exclusively serving the alarm and life-safety security dealer industry. name: Healthcare electronic medical records
tagline: Electronic medical records and clinical documentation software for healthcare providers
desc: Electronic medical records (EMR) software is the clinical core of healthcare IT — capturing, storing and displaying the longitudinal patient health record including clinical documentation, medication records, problem lists, allergies, lab results and imaging reports. Harris's Altera Digital Health is a major US EMR provider serving large health systems. MEDHOST serves community hospitals. QuadraMed has a long history serving acute care hospitals. dbMotion provides health information exchange.
why: EMR systems are legally mandated systems of record. Patient data stored in proprietary formats cannot be accessed without the original system. Hospital EHR migrations average 3-5 years and cost $50M+.
buyers: Hospital CIOs, CMIOs, health system CTOs, ambulatory care IT directors

AMT-Sybex (Jonas) provides utilities and infrastructure CMMS capabilities.
why: CMMS systems hold decades of maintenance histories, equipment calibration records and regulatory inspection documentation. These records are legally required for regulated assets.
buyers: Maintenance managers, reliability engineers, plant managers, facilities directors

name: Court
tagline: Court case management and judicial administration software
desc: Court management software provides case management, scheduling, document management, fee collection and reporting for courts, tribunals and judicial systems. CourtView (Volaris) is the leading court case management platform in North America, serving hundreds of courts from municipal to state supreme courts.
why: Court case management systems are legally designated systems of record. Any migration must maintain complete chain of custody for every case — requiring judicial oversight.
buyers: Court administrators, IT heads at court systems, judicial council technology directors

cos.name: CourtView Justice Solutions Inc.
cos.entity_desc: Volaris subsidiary/legal entity aligned to Public sector / justice / civic software, governed in Delaware. A search link is supplied because no direct entity website was confidently identified in the provided source files.

name: Creative agencies
tagline: Project management and workflow software for creative and advertising agencies
desc: Creative agency software manages the project lifecycle for advertising agencies, design studios and marketing consultancies — from briefing and scoping through creative production, approval workflow, billing and financial reporting. Volaris's Decideware and Aprais serve the agency management and performance measurement aspects of this vertical.
why: Creative agency management systems hold historical project data, client approval records and performance benchmarks that form the basis of agency compensation discussions.
buyers: Agency operations heads, creative directors, client services managers

cos.name: Decideware, Inc.
cos.entity_desc: Volaris subsidiary/legal entity aligned to Advertising / marketing / martech, governed in California. Direct website match available from the provided source files.

name: Credit unions
tagline: Core banking and member management software for credit unions
desc: Credit union software provides core banking, member management, loan origination, share and deposit management, and regulatory reporting for credit unions and mutual savings banks. PortfolioPlus (Volaris) serves Canadian financial institutions including credit unions with portfolio management software.
why: Core banking systems hold every member account and transaction. Any migration is systemically risky, requiring parallel running for 6-12 months and regulatory approval.
buyers: Credit union IT heads, CFOs, operations directors

name: Document management
tagline: Enterprise document management, records management and ECM software
desc: Document management software provides the systems for capturing, storing, organising, securing and retrieving documents and records across an organisation's lifecycle. Vela's Interica provides information management and document management solutions for mining, oil and gas and engineering industries — handling technical documents, project records and regulatory submissions.
why: Document management systems hold legally controlled documents with revision histories and approval chains. Any migration risks breaking the audit trail required for regulatory compliance.
buyers: Records managers, CISOs, document control managers, project document controllers

cos.name: Interica Limited
cos.entity_desc: Vela subsidiary/legal entity aligned to Mining / geoscience / oil and gas, governed in England and Wales. Direct website match available from the provided source files.

name: ESG
tagline: Environmental, social and governance reporting and sustainability management software
desc: ESG software helps organisations measure, manage and report their environmental, social and governance performance — from carbon accounting and climate risk assessment through supply chain due diligence, diversity metrics and governance transparency reporting. Vela's Envirosoft provides environmental compliance and reporting software for industrial and public sector organisations.
why: ESG platforms become the authoritative source for regulatory disclosures. Any migration risks data integrity of reported figures used in securities filings and supply chain compliance certifications.
buyers: Chief Sustainability Officers, ESG directors, compliance heads, VP EHS

cos.name: Envirosoft Corporation
cos.entity_desc: Vela subsidiary/legal entity aligned to Public sector / utilities / healthcare, governed in Alberta. Direct website match available from the provided source files.

name: Gaming
tagline: Gaming platform software for interactive entertainment and media companies
desc: Gaming software in the CSI portfolio relates to interactive entertainment and gaming technology platforms. Lumine's Wiztivi provides HTML5 middleware for interactive TV applications and gaming experiences on connected TV platforms. As the convergence of broadcasting and gaming accelerates, operators need specialised platforms that combine linear TV with interactive and gaming experiences.
why: Gaming middleware integrates with operator set-top box and connected TV platform deployments across millions of devices. Any replacement requires re-certifying every device model and rebuilding all application logic.
buyers: CTO at broadcasters, cable operators, interactive entertainment companies

cos.name: Wiztivi SAS
cos.entity_desc: Lumine subsidiary/legal entity aligned to Media / Advertising / Broadcast, governed in France. A search link is supplied because no direct entity website was confidently identified in the provided source files.

name: Human capital
tagline: Human capital management, workforce planning and HR analytics software
desc: Human capital management software covers the full employee lifecycle — from recruitment and onboarding through performance management, learning & development, succession planning, compensation management and workforce analytics. Vela serves this vertical through specialised HCM platforms in specific geographies and industries.
why: HCM systems hold every employee record, performance history and compensation data. Integration with payroll and operational scheduling creates complex dependencies making switching a major HR transformation programme.
buyers: CHRO, VP HR, HR IT heads, workforce analytics managers

name: Insurance
tagline: Insurance management, policy administration and claims software
desc: Insurance software provides the core technology for insurance carriers — managing policy administration, underwriting, claims management, actuarial reserving, reinsurance accounting and regulatory compliance. Vela's Financial Risk Solutions provides actuarial reserving and solvency management software for European insurance companies, helping them comply with Solvency II regulations.
why: Insurance policy systems hold the legal contracts for millions of policyholders. Migration requires re-validating every in-force policy and maintaining continuous claims-paying capability.
buyers: Insurance company CIOs, actuaries, VP Claims, compliance directors

name: Revenue cycle management
tagline: Healthcare revenue cycle management and medical billing software
desc: Revenue cycle management software manages the financial processes of healthcare providers — from patient registration and charge capture through claims submission, payment posting, denial management and patient collections. MEDHOST (Harris) and Altera Digital Health include integrated RCM capabilities serving community hospitals and health systems.
why: RCM systems process all provider revenue. Any disruption to claims submission or payment posting immediately impacts cash flow. Migration during a full fiscal year is extremely rare.
buyers: CFO, VP Revenue Cycle at hospitals and health systems, billing managers

cos.name: MEDHOST, Inc.
cos.entity_desc: Harris subsidiary/legal entity aligned to Healthcare, governed in Delaware. Direct website match available from the provided source files.

name: Third party logistics warehouse management systems

desc: Third-party logistics (3PL) warehouse management systems manage the complex multi-client operations of outsourced logistics providers — handling multiple clients' inventory, billing, EDI integration, labour management and client-specific processes. why: 3PL WMS systems hold all client inventory records and EDI configurations contractually committed to logistics customers. Migration requires simultaneously migrating every client's configuration and inventory.
buyers: 3PL operations heads, VP Technology at logistics companies

cos.name: InTempo Software
cos.entity_desc: Rental management ERP for local and regional independent equipment, tool, event and specialty rental operators (1–15 locations, often family-owned). Products: InTempo Enterprise (RentalMan) — full ERP on IBM i/RPG covering contracts, reservations, inventory, accounting, maintenance, dispatch and reporting; InTempo Core (Enfinity) — lighter ERP for smaller operators; CurbStone — rental-specific payment processing; InTempo CTX — AI-driven route/load optimisation (Wise Systems partnership); InTempo MX — connected assets/telematics. ~70% of customers start as family-run businesses. SaaS-hosted majority. Segments: heavy construction, aerial/access, general tools, party/event, specialty. Near-term AI opportunity: Mobile AI Damage Detection (photo before/after comparison on equipment return — customer demand validated at Nov 2025 user conference). Established 2015 by Volaris combining Solutions by Computer and Wynne Systems mid-market. Indianapolis, IN. Shared codebase with sister VBU Wynne Systems.

name: Winery management
tagline: Winery ERP, vineyard management, compliance and DTC sales software
desc:
Constellation Software entered the winery vertical in October 2007 when it acquired Orion Wine Software — then operating the WiPS, WIMS and Blend products that had served global wine enterprises since 1991. In June 2008, Orion expanded by acquiring Vintage Consulting Group, deepening its vineyard-management capabilities.

This followed Constellation's core strategy articulated by founder Mark Leonard: identify mission-critical vertical software with high switching costs, a small enough TAM to deter large competitors, and recurring revenue — then hold forever. CSI evaluates each acquisition using the First Chicago Method — a four-scenario weighted DCF (winner, modest winner, walking wounded, wipe-out) with a single hurdle rate (25–30%).

Over the next decade, the winery portfolio deepened under Jonas Software and its subsidiary CORA Group. In 2018, CORA consolidated Orion, adding vinSUITE for DTC commerce. In June 2024, Jonas added Ezy Systems — a comprehensive winery ERP for Australia.

Note: vintrace is independent of CSI and sits under Encompass Technologies. Constellation Brands (NYSE: STZ) is a separate, unrelated beverage company.
why:
Winery software sits at the intersection of agricultural complexity, stringent regulatory compliance and luxury direct-to-consumer retail — a combination no generic ERP handles well. CSI's winery stack mirrors its classic acquisition logic: Orion (Blend/WIMS/WiPS/DarWine) manages grape-to-bottle production and wholesale; vinSUITE captures DTC revenue through club, eCommerce and tasting room POS; Ezy Systems provides full-lifecycle ERP from vineyard through distributor. Switching costs are exceptionally high — Ridge Vineyards has used WiPS for over 27 years. Government compliance automation, which reduces reporting from days to minutes, creates further lock-in. The First Chicago Method's four-scenario DCF allows CSI to price these specialised niche businesses below where private equity can justify entry, giving CSI a structural advantage as permanent owner.
◆ Key Strategy: Why CSI Targets Small TAMs

Constellation Software deliberately seeks markets where the Total Addressable Market (TAM) is too small to attract large horizontal software vendors, but large enough to sustain multiple profitable, mission-critical incumbents.

The global winery software market is approximately $1.8B — a ceiling that deters Microsoft, Salesforce and Oracle from investing in a TTB-compliant, three-tier-aware, Brix-tracking ERP. Yet this same market is large enough to generate 25–30%+ EBITDA margins for the specialist who owns it.

This is the core of Mark Leonard's insight: "the most successful venture investments were vertical market software companies — each too small for big competitors to rationalise, yet with all the business virtues Buffett would want in a competitive moat."

For the winery vertical specifically: thousands of wineries need specialised software but no single winery is large enough to justify a custom build. The result is a fragmented installed base served by a handful of incumbents — exactly the consolidation opportunity CSI prices with its First Chicago hurdle. Each acquisition (Orion, vinSUITE, Ezy, WineFetch) was individually too small for private equity to bother, but collectively they cover the entire winery value chain permanently inside the Jonas ecosystem.
🏛 Key Regulatory Bodies & Acronyms
TTB — Alcohol and Tobacco Tax and Trade Bureau
The US federal agency (part of the Treasury Department) that regulates the production, labelling, advertising and taxation of alcoholic beverages. Every US winery must register with the TTB, obtain a Basic Permit, file Operational Reports (monthly or quarterly), pay federal excise tax (FET) on all wine removed from bond, and submit formula approvals for non-standard ingredients. Non-compliance carries significant fines and permit revocation. Orion's WiPS automates TTB report generation — what might take a compliance officer several days is reduced to minutes.
ABC — Alcoholic Beverage Control
State-level licensing and enforcement agencies that regulate the sale, distribution and consumption of alcohol within each US state. There are 50 separate ABC frameworks — each with its own rules on licences, permitted shipping states (only 47 states allow direct wine shipping), three-tier compliance, and reporting requirements. vinSUITE and Orion's WiPS embed state-by-state DTC shipping compliance directly into the checkout and fulfilment workflow.
Three-Tier System
The US regulatory framework — a legacy of post-Prohibition law — that separates alcohol production, distribution and retail into three legally distinct tiers. A winery (Tier 1) cannot sell directly to a retailer (Tier 3) without routing through a licensed distributor (Tier 2). DarWine (Orion's depletion reporting module) tracks wholesale inventory moving through distributors — providing the winery with real-time sell-through visibility.
FET — Federal Excise Tax
A per-gallon tax levied by the TTB on wine removed from a bonded winery premises. Rates vary by wine type and producer size (small wineries qualify for a reduced rate on the first 250,000 gallons). Accurate FET calculation requires precise inventory tracking of bonded stock — which WIMS manages automatically, including tax-class categorisation per lot.
DTC — Direct to Consumer
Sales made directly from a winery to the end consumer — via the tasting room, wine club, eCommerce website or allocation list — bypassing the three-tier distribution system. DTC is the highest-margin revenue channel (often 60–80% gross margin vs. 30–40% on wholesale) but carries the highest regulatory burden. vinSUITE automates DTC compliance at checkout, blocking shipments to unlicensed states and calculating per-state tax obligations in real time.
Wine Club
A recurring subscription model where members pre-commit to receive wine shipments per year, typically quarterly or bi-annually. Wine clubs are the cornerstone of winery DTC economics — members spend 3–5× more annually than one-time tasting room visitors. Managing wine club requires: member profile management, shipment scheduling, credit card decline handling, state compliance per shipment, and churn prediction (where analytics like vinSIGHT aim to intervene before a cancellation).
Brix
A measurement of dissolved sugar content in grape juice or must. Brix is the primary field metric harvest managers use to determine optimal picking date — typically 22–26° Brix for table wine grapes. Ezy Systems and Orion's Blend module track Brix readings by vineyard block and lot throughout harvest, feeding directly into fermentation scheduling and alcohol-yield projections.
Bonded Winery / Bond
A US TTB designation that grants a winery the right to produce, store and transfer wine before paying federal excise tax. All FET is deferred until wine is 'removed from bond.' WIMS tracks bonded vs. taxpaid inventory separately, ensuring accurate FET liability reporting and preventing costly TTB audit findings.
Lot / Lot Number
A unique identifier assigned to a specific batch of wine — tied to a vineyard block, grape variety, harvest date and production run. Lot tracking is the spine of winery production software: every movement (crush, ferment, blend, barrel aging, bottling) is logged against a lot, creating the complete chain of custody required for TTB records. Blend (Orion) and Ezy Systems both manage lot-level traceability from grape intake through finished goods.
VMS — Vertical Market Software
Software built exclusively for a single industry niche. Winery VMS is the archetype of CSI's acquisition thesis: the TAM is small enough (≈$1.8B globally) to deter Microsoft, Salesforce or Oracle, yet large enough to sustain multiple profitable incumbents with 25–30%+ EBITDA margins. Mission-critical workflows and regulatory lock-in create switching costs that translate into high net revenue retention — exactly the profile CSI prices with its First Chicago four-scenario DCF.
📋 Production & Vineyard Jargon
AVA — American Viticultural Area
A federally designated wine grape-growing region recognised by the TTB. Placing an AVA on a wine label requires that at least 85% of the grapes used come from that AVA — a claim traceable back to lot-level vineyard records. Orion's Blend and WIMS track grape origin by vineyard block, automatically validating AVA eligibility at blending and bottling.
COLA — Certificate of Label Approval
A mandatory TTB approval required before any new wine label can legally be used in interstate commerce. A mid-sized winery releasing 20–30 SKUs annually can manage hundreds of active COLAs. Winery ERP systems track COLA status per SKU, preventing sales teams from taking orders on wines whose labels have not yet been approved — a common and costly compliance failure.
Crush / Harvest Season
The annual period — typically August through November in the Northern Hemisphere — when grapes are harvested, sorted, crushed and pressed into juice. Crush is the highest-intensity operational period: incoming fruit volumes, lab testing, fermentation tank allocation, yeast additions and SO₂ management must all be tracked simultaneously across dozens of lots. Ezy Systems and Orion's Blend are purpose-built for crush throughput.
Must
The unfermented or actively-fermenting mixture of crushed grape juice, skins, seeds and pulp. Must tracking is the starting point of lot-level traceability. Blend tracks must volumes, sugar levels and yeast additions by fermentation vessel, enabling winemakers to monitor progress across multiple simultaneous fermentations.
Grower / Grower Payment
An independent vineyard owner who sells grapes under contract to a winery. Grower contracts specify price per ton (with bonuses for Brix targets or penalties for defects), payment schedule and delivery windows. Orion's Blend automates grower payment calculations, supporting complex structures including multi-tier bonuses, deductions and allowances across hundreds of grower accounts.
Varietal / Vintage
Varietal refers to the grape variety used — a varietal-labelled wine must contain at least 75% of that grape in the US. Vintage is the year the grapes were harvested — if stated on the label, US law requires 95% of the wine to come from that year's harvest. Both attributes are captured at lot creation and flow through every production, inventory and compliance record.
Barrel / Tank Inventory
Wine ages in barrels (typically 225-litre French or American oak) or stainless steel tanks. Barrel inventory management tracks which wine is in which vessel, cellar location, fill date, top-up history and projected racking date. WIMS maintains a real-time cellar map, calculates evaporation losses (the "angel's share"), and generates racking schedules.
Racking
The process of pumping or siphoning wine from one vessel to another to separate it from sediment (lees), introduce controlled oxygen exposure, or blend lots. Each racking event creates a new lot record, records volume losses, and updates barrel/tank location. Compliance records require every volume adjustment to be documented, making racking logs a key audit trail.
SKU — Stock Keeping Unit
A unique code assigned to each distinct product variant — defined by wine name, vintage, varietal, bottle format and packaging configuration. A winery with 15 wines across 3 bottle sizes has 45 SKUs. WIMS and WiPS manage SKU-level inventory, pricing, and order fulfilment, enabling accurate available-to-promise calculations across wholesale and DTC channels simultaneously.
📦 Sales & Distribution Jargon
Depletion Report
A sales report provided by a wholesale distributor showing how much of the winery's product has sold through to retail accounts and restaurants. DarWine (Orion) is purpose-built to ingest, reconcile and analyse depletion reports from multiple distributors across multiple states — consolidating fragmented data into brand-level performance visibility.
Allocation
A controlled distribution mechanism for high-demand, limited-production wines. Allocation management in vinSUITE handles: invitation emails, purchase windows, per-member quantity limits, waitlist management, and payment capture — ensuring that limited releases reach the winery's most loyal customers while preventing overselling.
POS — Point of Sale
The transaction system used in a winery's tasting room to process sales. Winery POS must handle wine club member lookup and benefits application, track per-visit spend for club retention scoring, enforce state ID verification, and capture wine preferences for future marketing. vinSUITE's tasting room POS integrates directly with its club management module, so a tasting room purchase updates the member's profile in real time.
Tasting Room
The retail and hospitality space where wineries receive visitors for wine tastings, tours and direct sales. The tasting room is typically the highest-margin revenue channel and the primary recruiting ground for wine club members. Tasting room performance metrics include: visitors per day, conversion rate to purchase, average transaction value, club sign-ups per tasting, and revenue per visitor.
Open Order / Backorder
An open order is a wholesale or DTC sales order placed but not yet fulfilled. A backorder arises when inventory is insufficient to ship. WiPS tracks open order status, links orders to production lot availability, and generates pick-and-pack instructions when inventory is confirmed.
💰 Financial & Compliance Jargon
Operations Report (OR)
The mandatory TTB monthly or quarterly filing that reports every volume movement at a bonded winery: wine produced, received, transferred, bottled, sold and ending inventory by wine type. A single error can trigger a TTB audit. Orion's WiPS and WIMS auto-generate OR data directly from production and inventory transactions, eliminating 2–5 days per month of manual reconciliation.
Wine Excise Tax (State-level)
In addition to federal FET, each US state levies its own excise tax on wine — rates range from $0.11 per litre (California) to over $2.00 per litre in some states. vinSUITE's checkout engine applies the correct state excise tax at the point of order, files combined reports, and maintains an audit trail for each state's revenue authority.
Nexus
The legal concept determining whether a winery has sufficient presence in a state to trigger an obligation to collect and remit sales tax. For DTC wine shippers, a direct-shipping licence typically establishes nexus, requiring the winery to register as a tax collector. vinSUITE handles nexus obligations across 47 shipping states through automated state tax compliance engines.
🍇 Wine Club Jargon
Club Tier
Most wineries offer multiple club membership levels (2-bottle, 4-bottle, 6-bottle, mixed, red-only, white-only, reserve) each with different price points, benefits and shipment frequencies. vinSUITE manages club tier configuration, automates the selection of wines per tier per shipment, and handles member upgrades and downgrades without manual intervention.
Churn
The rate at which wine club members cancel their memberships — industry average is approximately 30–35% annually. Reducing churn by even 5 percentage points has a significant compound effect on DTC revenue. Predictive churn analytics (like vinSIGHT) aim to identify at-risk members before they cancel, enabling targeted retention interventions.
Skip / Skip Hold
A wine club member's request to defer a shipment without cancelling their membership. vinSUITE tracks skip frequency per member — a member who has skipped 2+ consecutive shipments is a strong churn signal, feeding into retention scoring and targeted outreach workflows.
Pickup Club
A wine club model where members collect their shipment in person at the tasting room. Pickup clubs are common in Napa and Sonoma — they generate higher margin (no shipping cost) and drive repeat tasting room visits. Winery POS and club management systems must distinguish pickup vs. shipped members, track pickup status, and trigger follow-up communications.
♞ CSI Strategic Fit — Why Winery Is a Textbook Constellation Play Winery management software is almost a textbook fit for Constellation's "tiny, mission-critical verticals + First Chicago valuation" playbook. The vertical scores highly on every dimension CSI screens for: switching costs, TAM size, recurring revenue, and competitive insularity.

1. Vertical, Mission-Critical, High Switching Costs
Orion, Ezy Systems and vinSUITE are built exclusively for wineries — not for general manufacturing or generic retail. These systems run the core workflows that keep a winery legal and profitable: grape intake, production, inventory, compliance reporting and all sales channels. Ripping one out would disrupt operations, invalidate TTB audit trails, and require re-training every department simultaneously. Ridge Vineyards' 27+ year continuous use of WiPS is a real-world data point on how sticky this software actually is. A winery that loses its TTB permit ceases to exist — making this software mission-critical for staying legal, not just operationally convenient.

2. Small TAM, Low Competition, Recurring Revenue
The addressable market is thousands of wineries — not millions of generic SMBs — which is exactly the "too small for big competitors to care" TAM profile CSI targets. Microsoft, Salesforce and Oracle have no strategic rationale to build a TTB-compliant, three-tier-aware, Brix-tracking production ERP when the global winery software market is approximately $1.8 billion. That ceiling is CSI's floor. Their commercial models — subscription, licence-plus-maintenance, and SaaS ERP — naturally generate recurring revenue streams. vinSUITE's wine club platform adds a second layer: as winery wine clubs grow, vinSUITE's transaction volumes and per-member fees grow proportionally.

3. How the First Chicago Lens Applies to Winery VMS
CSI uses the First Chicago Method — a four-scenario probability-weighted DCF (winner, modest winner, walking wounded, wipe-out) with a 25–30% hurdle rate — to price every acquisition. Here is how the four scenarios map to the winery vertical:
Winner: Continued dominance in enterprise winery ERP, gradual module expansion (analytics, club AI, cloud migration revenue), modest price increases across a sticky base.
Modest Winner: Flat to low-growth ARR with stable margins. The core installed base renews reliably, throwing off strong free cash flow at a low reinvestment rate — exactly what CSI's permanent capital model is built to hold.
Walking Wounded: Some wineries defect to newer cloud rivals, trimming growth and margins but leaving a durable legacy base. Even a well-funded competitor takes years to poach a compliance-embedded installed base.
Wipe-Out: Regulatory or technology shocks erode the vertical materially. Given the niche's insularity, this scenario carries the lowest probability weight.

4. Why CSI Specifically Likes This Vertical
Operationally, winery software lets Constellation deploy its full playbook: cross-selling DTC, production and ERP modules to the same customer base; incremental price and feature improvements compounding over decades; and bolt-on acquisitions (WineFetch for eCommerce, Ezy for antipodean ERP) that extend wallet share without disrupting existing customers.

The sub-group compounding chain: Orion (CORA Group → Jonas → CSI) handles production and wholesale; vinSUITE (Jonas → CSI) owns DTC; Ezy Systems (Jonas → CSI) adds geographic coverage in Australia — collectively covering more of the winery value chain than any single competitor could have reached organically.
